description: Operational system of record from which this supply plan record originated. Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; Oracle Cloud ERP

description: Indicates whether this supply plan record reflects a constrained supply scenario where planned production or receipts are insufficient to fully satisfy planned demand. Triggers allocation decision workflows.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Net supply-demand balance position for the SKU over the planning horizon (planned supply minus planned demand). Positive values indicate surplus; negative values indicate a supply gap requiring allocation decisions.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Base unit of measure for all quantity fields in this supply plan record (e.g., CS=Case, EA=Each, KG=Kilogram). Aligns with the SKU's base UOM in SAP S/4HANA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|OZ — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM (Materials Management); ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: Timestamp when this supply plan record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks revisions and supports audit trail for IBP governance.
reference: SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Sequential integer version number of this supply plan snapshot within a planning cycle. Increments with each revision, enabling plan-vs-plan variance analysis.

business_glossary_term: Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) Cycle ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for the S&OP/Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle record. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete, recurring cross-functional planning event with a defined lifecycle (draft → in-progress → closed).

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: S&OP financial reconciliation step compares consensus plan to the financial budget; the sop_cycle references the budget version being reconciled. F&B S&OP explicitly includes a financial reconciliatio

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: S&OP cycles are aligned to fiscal periods; the financial reconciliation step of S&OP requires linking the cycle to the fiscal period being planned. F&B S&OP practitioners always run cycles within a fi

Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: In global F&B, S&OP cycles are market-scoped — consensus_demand_volume, revenue_gap_amount, and service_level_target_pct are market-specific outputs. A domain expert expects sop_cycle to reference its

Total number of cross-functional participants who attended the S&OP/IBP cycle meetings (demand review, supply review, pre-S&OP, and executive review combined). Used as a process maturity indicator and governance compliance metric.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A IBP — Meeting Governance

description: Aggregate manufacturing capacity utilization percentage (0–100) for the planning period as assessed during the supply review of this S&OP cycle. Derived from the constrained supply plan vs. available production capacity. Values above 85% typically trigger capacity escalation discussions.
reference: SAP IBP — Capacity Planning; Aveva/Wonderware MES — OEE Analytics

description: Free-text narrative summarizing the key cross-functional decisions, trade-offs, and commitments agreed upon during this S&OP/IBP cycle. Captures the rationale for scenario selection, supply gap resolutions, and any deviations from the financial operating plan.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A IBP — Decision Documentation; SAP IBP

description: Unit of measure for the consensus demand volume approved in this S&OP cycle (e.g., CS = cases, EA = each, KG = kilograms). Ensures consistent interpretation of volume figures across demand, supply, and finance teams. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP — UOM; GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standards

description: Total consensus demand volume (in standard cases or agreed unit of measure) approved by cross-functional teams during this S&OP cycle. Represents the agreed-upon demand signal used to drive supply planning, procurement, and manufacturing scheduling for the planning period.
reference: SAP IBP — Consensus Demand; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Financial revenue target (in reporting currency) aligned and approved during this S&OP/IBP cycle. Represents the commercial commitment reconciled between the demand plan and the financial operating plan. Used for EBITDA tracking and financial close alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Profitability Analysis; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: Date and time when this S&OP cycle record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage, compliance reporting, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 22000 — Record Keeping; FSMA — Traceability Records

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this S&OP cycle record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency global operations.
reference: ISO 4217 — Currency Codes; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: Date and time when this S&OP/IBP cycle was formally closed and the approved consensus plan was locked for execution. Marks the end of the active planning window. Used for cycle duration KPI measurement and audit compliance.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Cycle Closure; Oliver Wight IBP

description: Free-text field for additional commentary, risk flags, assumptions, or contextual notes recorded by the S&OP cycle owner or cross-functional team. May include references to external market events, supply disruptions, regulatory changes, or strategic initiatives impacting the planning cycle.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A IBP — Assumptions Documentation

description: Externally-known business identifier for the S&OP/IBP cycle, formatted as SOP-YYYY-MM-NNN (e.g., SOP-2024-10-001). Used in cross-functional communications, meeting agendas, and audit trails to uniquely reference a planning cycle. Maps to SAP IBP Planning Cycle ID.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Cycle; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Date and time when this S&OP/IBP cycle was formally opened and made active in the planning system. Serves as the principal business event timestamp marking the start of the cycle's active planning window. Used for SLA tracking and cycle duration analytics.

Current lifecycle state of the S&OP/IBP cycle. Drives workflow routing, access controls, and downstream planning system locks. Draft: cycle initiated but not yet active. In Progress: milestone reviews underway. Pending Approval: awaiting executive sign-off. Approved: consensus plan ratified. Closed: cycle archived. Cancelled: cycle voided.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Cycle Status; Oliver Wight IBP Maturity Model

description: Classification of the planning cycle cadence and scope. Determines which cross-functional teams participate and which decisions are in scope. Monthly S&OP covers full supply-demand balancing; weekly demand review focuses on near-term demand sensing; executive IBP covers strategic financial reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monthly_sop|weekly_demand_review|executive_ibp|supply_review|pre_sop|demand_review — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP — IBP for S&OP; Oliver Wight Class A S&OP Framework

description: Version identifier of the demand plan (from SAP IBP or JDA/Blue Yonder) that was reviewed and approved during this S&OP cycle. Enables traceability between the S&OP cycle record and the specific demand plan version used as input to supply planning and financial reconciliation.
reference: SAP IBP — Plan Version Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Scheduled or actual date of the Demand Review milestone within this S&OP cycle. During the demand review, commercial and demand planning teams align on the unconstrained consensus demand forecast, incorporating statistical forecasts, market intelligence, and promotional uplifts.
reference: SAP IBP — S&OP Demand Review; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Scheduled or actual date of the Executive S&OP or IBP review meeting where senior leadership approves the consensus plan, resolves escalated supply-demand gaps, and aligns on financial commitments and strategic trade-offs.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A IBP; SAP IBP — Executive IBP Review

description: Status of the financial reconciliation between the consensus volume plan and the financial operating plan (budget/latest estimate) at the close of this S&OP cycle. Aligned: volume plan supports financial targets. Gap Identified: volume shortfall vs. financial plan. Escalated: gap referred to CFO/leadership. Resolved: gap closed via scenario or plan adjustment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Profitability Analysis; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials; Oliver Wight IBP Financial Integration

description: The fiscal period (month number 1–13 depending on calendar) within the fiscal year to which this S&OP cycle belongs. Enables alignment of planning cycle outcomes with period-close financial reporting and EBITDA reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Fiscal Period; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: The fiscal year to which this S&OP cycle belongs, as defined in the corporate financial calendar (e.g., 2024). Used for financial reconciliation, budget alignment, and year-over-year planning comparisons.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Fiscal Year Variant; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: Target days of supply (DOS) for finished goods inventory approved during this S&OP cycle. Reflects the inventory optimization policy balancing service level targets, working capital constraints, and shelf life (FEFO) requirements for the planning period.
reference: SAP IBP — Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Inventory Planning

description: Indicates whether this S&OP cycle's consensus plan has been locked for execution (True), preventing further modifications to the approved demand and supply plan. Locking triggers downstream replenishment signals, procurement purchase orders, and manufacturing schedules.
reference: SAP IBP — Plan Lock; JDA/Blue Yonder Planning Freeze

description: Number of new product development (NPD) SKUs included in the demand and supply plans reviewed during this S&OP cycle. Tracks the volume of innovation pipeline items requiring dedicated supply chain readiness assessment and launch planning.
reference: SAP IBP — New Product Introduction; PLM Integration

description: Number of open action items carried forward from this S&OP cycle at the time of cycle closure. Tracks cross-functional accountability and process discipline. A high count may indicate unresolved supply-demand gaps or escalation items requiring follow-up in the next cycle.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A S&OP — Action Item Tracking

description: Number of months covered by the planning horizon reviewed in this S&OP cycle. Typically 18–24 months for strategic IBP cycles and 3–6 months for operational S&OP cycles in the Food and Beverage industry. Drives the time fence for supply-demand balancing decisions.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Horizon; Oliver Wight Class A IBP

description: Last calendar date of the planning horizon governed by this S&OP cycle. Together with planning_period_start_date, defines the full time fence for supply-demand balancing decisions made in this cycle.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Horizon; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: First calendar date of the planning horizon governed by this S&OP cycle. Defines the start boundary for demand, supply, inventory, and financial plans reviewed and approved within this cycle. Typically the first day of the planning month or week.
reference: SAP IBP — Planning Horizon; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Scheduled or actual date of the Pre-S&OP (reconciliation) meeting where supply, demand, and finance teams resolve gaps between the demand plan and constrained supply plan before escalating unresolved issues to the executive S&OP review.
reference: Oliver Wight Class A S&OP; SAP IBP — Pre-S&OP Step

description: Percentage of total consensus demand volume attributable to trade promotions and promotional uplifts in the planning period reviewed during this S&OP cycle. High promotional volume percentages increase demand variability and require additional safety stock and supply flexibility.
reference: SAP IBP — Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder TPM Integration

description: Monetary gap (in reporting currency) between the consensus revenue plan approved in this S&OP cycle and the financial operating plan (budget or latest estimate). Positive values indicate revenue shortfall vs. financial plan. Key input for executive escalation and corrective action planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Variance Analysis; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

description: Indicates whether safety stock parameters were formally reviewed and updated during this S&OP cycle (True) or carried forward unchanged from the prior cycle (False). Safety stock reviews are triggered by significant changes in demand variability, lead times, or service level targets.
reference: SAP IBP — Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Inventory Planning

description: Name or label of the supply-demand scenario approved by the executive team during this S&OP cycle (e.g., 'Base Case', 'Upside Demand', 'Supply Constrained'). Identifies which planning scenario was selected as the consensus plan for execution.
reference: SAP IBP — Scenario Planning; Oliver Wight IBP Scenario Management

description: Classification of the approved planning scenario for this S&OP cycle. Base Case: most likely outcome. Upside: optimistic demand/supply scenario. Downside: pessimistic scenario. Supply Constrained: demand exceeds available supply. Risk Adjusted: incorporates identified supply chain risk mitigations.
reference: SAP IBP — Scenario Planning; Oliver Wight Class A IBP

description: Target customer service level (fill rate or OTIF percentage) approved for the planning period during this S&OP cycle. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.50 = 98.5%). Drives safety stock parameters and replenishment signal calibration across the DC network.
reference: SAP IBP — Service Level Optimization; SLA Framework

description: Operational system of record from which this S&OP cycle record was sourced or initiated. SAP_IBP: SAP Integrated Business Planning. JDA_BLUE_YONDER: JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning. ORACLE_ERP: Oracle Cloud ERP. MANUAL: manually entered outside a planning system.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder; Oracle Cloud ERP

description: Volumetric gap (in consensus demand UOM) between the unconstrained consensus demand plan and the constrained supply plan identified during this S&OP cycle. Positive values indicate supply shortfall; negative values indicate supply surplus. Key input for executive escalation and scenario planning.
reference: SAP IBP — Supply-Demand Balancing; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Scheduled or actual date of the Supply Review milestone within this S&OP cycle. Supply planning teams assess capacity constraints, raw material availability, MOQ compliance, and FIFO/FEFO inventory rotation to produce a constrained supply plan.
reference: SAP IBP — S&OP Supply Review; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Date and time when this S&OP cycle record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer and audit trail requirements for planning governance.
reference: ISO 22000 — Record Keeping; SAP IBP — Change Log

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the replenishment order record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete supply chain planning event with a full lifecycle (planned → firmed → released → received).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module — Stock Transport Order / Planned Order

description: Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Replenishment Order Execution Report ties each order to the retailer account that generated demand, enabling OTIF performance tracking and cost allocation.

Foreign key linking to ingredient.approved_supplier. Business justification: F&B procurement policy mandates replenishment orders be placed only against approved supplier-ingredient combinations. Linking replenishment_order to approved_supplier enforces this constraint, suppor

Foreign key linking to procurement.approved_vendor_list. Business justification: F&B procurement compliance requires that replenishment orders reference the AVL record authorizing the supplier for that material. AVL status (approved, suspended) must be verified at order creation —

Foreign key linking to product.bill_of_materials. Business justification: Co-packing replenishment orders (co_packing_flag on replenishment_order) and MRP-driven orders reference the active BOM for component requirements, yield_percentage, and batch sizing. F&B supply plann
Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Promotional replenishment orders are created from active campaigns to ensure inventory availability for campaign‑driven sales.
Reference to the logistics carrier or 3PL partner assigned to transport this replenishment order. Used for carrier performance tracking, freight cost allocation, and SLA compliance monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Freight Vendor; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Replenishment Order Execution process requires identifying the target DC to restock; linking ensures accurate inventory allocation and DC‑level reporting.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assign order processing and procurement costs to a cost center for internal budgeting.

Reference to the demand plan or forecast that drove the generation of this replenishment order. Links supply execution back to the demand sensing or S&OP cycle, enabling supply-demand balancing analysis and forecast accuracy measurement.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Reference to the receiving plant, Distribution Center (DC), or 3PL facility where inventory is to be delivered. Drives inbound shipment creation, receiving dock scheduling, and inventory position updates at the destination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Receiving Plant; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Replenishment orders specify a destination storage location within the DC for WMS-directed putaway. While destination_location_network_node_id captures the node level, storage_location_id provides the

Foreign key linking to customer.edi_trading_partner. Business justification: In F&B, replenishment orders are frequently triggered and exchanged via EDI (850/855/856 transactions) with specific retail trading partners. This FK links each replenishment order to its EDI trading

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Replenishment orders are accrued within fiscal periods for COGS and inventory accounting. Finance teams tie replenishment order activity to fiscal periods for period-end accruals and inventory valuati

Foreign key linking to distribution.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Replenishment orders in supply planning trigger fulfillment orders in distribution execution — this is the core supply-to-distribution handoff in F&B DC operations. Linking them enables order lifecycl
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Record the financial impact of replenishment orders in the appropriate GL account.

Foreign key linking to ingredient.cost. Business justification: Replenishment orders for raw materials reference the approved cost record (standard/planned price) at order creation for financial commitment and purchase order valuation. F&B procurement requires cap

Foreign key linking to supply.lane. Business justification: Replenishment orders move finished goods between network nodes along defined transportation lanes. replenishment_order.transport_mode is an execution-specific attribute (kept as it captures the actual

Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_trace. Business justification: Replenishment orders for perishable F&B products must track lot/batch assignments to enforce FEFO rotation and support recall readiness. replenishment_order carries a denormalized batch_number; a prop

Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Replenishment orders are generated by MRP/supply planning as execution outputs of the supply plan. replenishment_order.ibp_plan_version is a string label referencing the IBP plan version, but not a st

Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Replenishment orders in F&B are fulfilled against specific PO lines (e.g., blanket PO releases). Line-level linkage enables accurate quantity tracking, shelf-life compliance per line, and invoice matc

Reference to the originating plant, Distribution Center (DC), or 3PL facility from which inventory will be shipped or transferred. Used for network design analysis, inbound logistics planning, and supply-demand balancing across the DC network.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Plant/Storage Location Master; JDA/Blue Yonder Network Design

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_standard_cost. Business justification: Replenishment order financial valuation (total_order_value, unit_cost) must reference the approved standard cost record for GL posting and cost variance reporting. F&B finance teams reconcile replenis

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: DRP/MRP replenishment signals trigger make-to-replenish production orders in F&B supply chains. Linking replenishment_order to production_order enables end-to-end replenishment fulfillment tracking, p

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Replenishment orders are charged to profit centers for product-line P&L reporting. F&B companies allocate replenishment costs to profit centers to track supply chain costs by brand or product line; co

Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Promotional demand drives replenishment orders in F&B supply chains. Supply planners must trace which replenishment orders were generated to fulfill a promotion plan's volume commitment, enabling prom

Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Replenishment orders are placed under governing purchase contracts that define price, MOQ, and delivery terms. Linking enables contract compliance tracking, volume commitment consumption monitoring, a
Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Replenishment orders are converted to external purchase orders; linking tracks the conversion and order lifecycle.

Reference to the finished good, raw material, or packaging material being replenished. Links to the material master for product specifications, shelf life parameters, and FIFO/FEFO rotation rules applicable to this order.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master (MARA/MARC); FDA 21 CFR Part 117

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.recall_event. Business justification: When a recall is issued, replenishment orders for affected SKUs must be halted immediately. Supply planners link orders to active recall events to prevent shipment of recalled product — a mandatory FS

Foreign key linking to supply.safety_stock_policy. Business justification: replenishment_order has a safety_stock_trigger BOOLEAN flag indicating the order was triggered by a safety stock policy breach. When safety_stock_trigger=true, the specific safety_stock_policy that tr

Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Order‑driven replenishment: each replenishment order is generated to satisfy a specific sales order, required for the Replenishment Execution Report.

Foreign key linking to product.shelf_life_spec. Business justification: Replenishment orders in F&B specify minimum remaining shelf life requirements (shelf_life_days_remaining field) governed by the shelf_life_spec's dc_receiving_standard and rotation_rule. Supply planne

Foreign key linking to customer.ship_to_location. Business justification: Replenishment orders in F&B (VMI, DSD, retail replenishment) are fulfilled to specific customer ship-to locations. The existing account_id identifies the customer but not the delivery point. This FK i

Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: REQUIRED: Replenishment order processing and regulatory traceability need a direct FK to the SKU; experts expect sku_id on order for cost, shelf‑life, and compliance reporting.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Order fulfillment process tracks which inventory stock position is being replenished, enabling OTIF and inventory aging analysis.

Foreign key linking to sales.store. Business justification: DSD (Direct Store Delivery) and VMI replenishment orders in F&B are fulfilled to specific store locations. Store-level replenishment is a core operational process; a domain expert expects replenishmen

Reference to the supplier or vendor party fulfilling this replenishment order for external procurement types (purchase requisition, purchase order). For internal stock transfers, this references the supplying plant or DC as a party. Maps to PARTY_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master (LFA1); TraceGains Supplier Compliance

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site. Business justification: FSMA requires traceability to the specific supplier manufacturing site for each replenishment order. Site-level data (lead time, food safety cert, capacity) affects order planning. replenishment_order

The date on which goods were physically received at the destination location (goods receipt posting date). Used to calculate On Time In Full (OTIF) performance, lead time actuals, and delivery variance against requested and confirmed dates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Receipt Date (MIGO); OTIF KPI

description: The Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) number transmitted by the supplier or source location confirming shipment of this replenishment order. Enables proactive receiving dock scheduling, WMS pre-receipt processing, and shipment tracking. Transmitted via EDI 856.
reference: EDI 856 ASN Standard; GS1 Supply Chain Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM — Inbound Delivery

description: The delivery date confirmed by the supplier or supplying location. May differ from requested_delivery_date. Used to calculate delivery date variance, assess OTIF risk, and trigger escalation workflows when confirmation is later than requested.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Confirmed Delivery Date; EDI 855

description: The quantity confirmed by the supplier or supplying location as available and committed for fulfillment. May differ from ordered_quantity due to partial availability, capacity constraints, or MOQ adjustments. Used for supply-demand gap analysis and OTIF measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Confirmed Quantity; EDI 855 Purchase Order Acknowledgment

description: Timestamp when this replenishment order record was first captured in the data platform. Distinct from order_date (business event time). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of data freshness. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA MM — Change Document

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit_cost and total_order_value are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and financial consolidation across global operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI — Currency Configuration

description: The expiration or best-before date of the batch being replenished. Used to enforce FEFO rotation policy, calculate shelf_life_days_remaining, and ensure compliance with customer minimum remaining shelf life requirements. Critical for food safety and waste reduction.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 117; Codex Alimentarius — Food Labeling; ISO 22000

description: The SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) plan version or S&OP cycle version under which this replenishment order was generated (e.g., 'BASELINE_2024Q3', 'CONSENSUS_V2'). Enables comparison of supply proposals across planning scenarios and IBP consensus cycles.
reference: SAP IBP — Integrated Business Planning; S&OP Process

description: The planned lead time in calendar days from order release to expected goods receipt at the destination location. Derived from the purchasing info record or supply planning parameters. Used for replenishment timing calculations, safety stock parameter setting, and supply planning optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record Lead Time; SAP IBP Inventory Optimization

description: Indicates whether the ordered_quantity meets or exceeds the Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) requirement for this material-supplier combination. True = compliant; False = below MOQ threshold, requiring buyer review or rounding up before release.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MOQ Check; SAP IBP Supply Planning

description: The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) applicable to this replenishment order as defined in the purchasing info record or supply planning parameters. Ensures ordered_quantity meets supplier or logistics minimum thresholds. Critical for procurement compliance and cost optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record (EINA/EINE); MOQ Policy

description: The MRP controller code or planner identifier responsible for managing the replenishment of this material at the source plant. Used for workload assignment, exception management, and accountability tracking in supply planning operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MRP Controller (DISPO); MD04 MRP List

description: Free-text field for operational notes, buyer comments, exception explanations, or special handling instructions associated with this replenishment order. Used by supply planners and procurement teams to document non-standard situations, escalations, or supplier communications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Order Text

description: The business date on which the replenishment order was created or generated by the MRP/supply planning system. Represents the principal real-world event time for this transaction. Used for aging analysis, lead time calculation, and OTIF measurement baseline. Maps to BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — ME21N Order Date; SAP IBP Demand Sensing

description: Externally-known business identifier for the replenishment order as assigned by the ERP or supply planning system (e.g., SAP purchase order number, stock transport order number, or MRP planned order number). Used for cross-system traceability and communication with suppliers and logistics partners. Maps to BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — ME21N / MD04; SAP IBP Supply Planning

description: Current lifecycle state of the replenishment order. Tracks progression from MRP-generated planned order through firming, release to warehouse/supplier, in-transit movement, and final goods receipt. Cancelled indicates the order was voided before fulfillment. Maps to LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Stock Transport Order Status; SAP IBP Supply Planning

description: Classification of the replenishment order by its procurement mechanism. Stock Transfer Order (STO) moves inventory between internal plants or DCs. Purchase Requisition is a request to procure from an external supplier. Planned Order is an MRP-generated supply proposal. Purchase Order is a confirmed external procurement. Intercompany Transfer moves goods between legal entities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stock_transfer_order|purchase_requisition|planned_order|purchase_order|intercompany_transfer — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Order Type Configuration; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: The quantity of material requested in the replenishment order, expressed in the base unit of measure (e.g., cases, pallets, kg). This is the gross supply proposal quantity generated by MRP or supply planning before any MOQ rounding or adjustment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Order Quantity (MENGE); SAP IBP Inventory Optimization

description: The planned date on which goods are to be issued from the source location (outbound shipment departure). Used for outbound logistics scheduling, warehouse pick/pack planning, and lead time calculation between goods issue and goods receipt.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Planned Goods Issue Date (STO); JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Reference to the MRP or supply planning run that generated this replenishment order. Enables traceability of supply proposals back to the specific planning cycle, demand signal, and parameter set used. Supports audit of planning decisions and scenario comparison.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MRP Run (MD01/MD02); SAP IBP Planning Run

description: Business priority assigned to the replenishment order to govern fulfillment sequencing when supply is constrained. Critical = immediate action required (e.g., stockout risk); High = elevated urgency; Medium = standard planning cycle; Low = opportunistic or forward-buy.
reference: SAP IBP Supply Planning — Priority; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Indicates whether this replenishment order or the associated batch is subject to a quality hold that prevents goods receipt or putaway at the destination. True = quality hold active; False = no hold. Integrates with QM module and HACCP/GMP compliance processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — Quality Hold; HACCP; GMP; ISO 22000; GFSI

description: The actual quantity of material received at the destination location upon goods receipt. Used to calculate fill rate, identify short shipments, and trigger OTIF performance measurement against the confirmed quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Goods Receipt (MIGO); OTIF Measurement

description: The date by which the replenishment order is required to arrive at the destination location to meet demand or safety stock targets. Drives supplier scheduling, transportation booking, and OTIF measurement. Set by supply planning based on demand signals and inventory parameters.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Delivery Date; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: The inventory rotation policy applicable to this replenishment order. FIFO (First In First Out) = oldest stock consumed first; FEFO (First Expired First Out) = stock with earliest expiry date consumed first (critical for perishable food and beverage products); LIFO (Last In First Out) = rarely used in food industry.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 117 — FSMA; GFSI; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Indicates whether this replenishment order was triggered by a safety stock breach (projected inventory falling below safety stock level) rather than a standard demand-driven replenishment signal. True = safety stock replenishment; False = demand-driven or scheduled replenishment.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization — Safety Stock; SAP S/4HANA MM — MRP Safety Stock

description: The number of days of remaining shelf life on the material at the time of order creation, based on the production date and expiry date of the batch being replenished. Critical for FEFO compliance, quality assurance, and ensuring product arrives at destination with sufficient remaining shelf life to meet customer requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 117; ISO 22000; GFSI — Shelf Life Management

description: The total monetary value of the replenishment order calculated as ordered_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Used for procurement spend analysis, inventory investment tracking, and financial planning. Expressed in the order currency. Classified as confidential.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Order Value; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The mode of transportation used to move the replenishment order from source to destination location. Drives freight cost estimation, lead time calculation, and carbon footprint reporting for sustainability compliance.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; EPA Environmental Compliance

description: The standard or purchase cost per unit of measure for the material in this replenishment order. Used to calculate total order value, Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) impact, and inventory valuation. Classified as confidential as it reflects procurement pricing strategy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Standard Cost; SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Info Record

description: The base unit of measure in which ordered, confirmed, and received quantities are expressed (e.g., CS=Cases, EA=Each, KG=Kilograms, LB=Pounds, PAL=Pallets, L=Liters, GAL=Gallons). Aligns with the material master UOM configuration in SAP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|PAL|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: GS1 Unit of Measure Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM — Base Unit of Measure (MEINS)

description: Timestamp when this replenishment order record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks changes to order quantity, status, dates, or routing. Used for incremental data processing and audit compliance. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA MM — Change Document

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the safety stock policy record. Primary key for the safety_stock_policy data product in the supply domain.
Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Safety‑stock policies are often set per brand to mitigate demand spikes generated by brand‑specific marketing promotions.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: In F&B, safety stock policies are elevated for specific marketing campaigns (is_promotional=true). Linking to the specific campaign that triggered the policy change enables campaign ROI analysis (incr

Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Sales contracts with minimum service level commitments (e.g., 98.5% fill rate) directly govern safety stock policy parameters. Contract-driven inventory policy management is a named F&B process — plan
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Track safety‑stock holding costs against the cost center responsible for inventory.

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_standard_cost. Business justification: Safety stock valuation: finance values safety stock inventory using standard costs; the policy defines the quantity, the standard cost record provides the unit value for balance sheet working capital

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Safety stock policies are reviewed and approved within fiscal periods; finance uses safety stock levels to calculate working capital requirements per period for balance sheet planning. effective_date

Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: Safety stock policies in global F&B are market-specific — service_level_target_pct, lead_time_days, and demand_variability_factor differ by market. A domain expert expects safety_stock_policy to be ma
Reference to the DC (Distribution Center) or warehouse location where this safety stock policy applies. Supports multi-node DC network and 3PL partner locations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM Storage Location; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning Network

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Safety‑stock calculations must respect product‑specific regulatory limits (e.g., maximum allowable stock for allergen‑controlled items).

Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: F&B supply planners create promotion-specific safety stock policies (is_promotional=true) tied to a specific promotion plan, setting elevated safety stock targets for SKUs during promotional windows.

Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: In F&B, safety stock policies are differentiated by customer segment — higher service level targets for key grocery accounts vs. foodservice or DTC. Linking segment_id enables segment-driven policy co

Foreign key linking to product.shelf_life_spec. Business justification: Safety stock policy configuration in F&B directly depends on shelf life specs: remaining_shelf_life_min_pct, rotation_policy (FEFO), and safety_days_of_supply are all governed by the shelf_life_spec.
Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) governed by this safety stock policy. Links to the product master to identify the specific finished good or semi-finished item.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; GS1 GTIN Standard

description: Foreign key linking to supply.sop_cycle. Business justification: Safety stock policies are reviewed, approved, and updated during S&OP cycles. The sop_cycle governs the IBP/S&OP process including safety_stock_review_flag. Adding sop_cycle_id to safety_stock_policy

Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Safety‑stock calculations depend on product specs (shelf‑life, storage) defined in SPECIFICATION; required for inventory planning reports.

Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Safety stock policies in F&B are defined at the storage location level because ambient, chilled, and frozen locations have fundamentally different lead times, variability factors, and shelf-life const

ABC classification of the SKU based on demand volume or revenue contribution. 'A' items are high-value/high-velocity requiring tighter safety stock management; 'C' items are low-velocity with broader buffers. Used to prioritize inventory management effort.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; APICS CPIM

description: Date on which this safety stock policy was formally approved by the designated supply chain authority. Part of the S&OP governance and change management audit trail.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; ISO 9001

description: Method used to derive the safety stock quantity. 'statistical' uses demand variability and service level targets; 'days_of_supply' uses a fixed days-of-supply multiplier; 'manual' is planner-set; 'min_max' uses min/max replenishment bands; 'demand_driven' uses demand sensing signals from SAP IBP.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; APICS CPIM

description: Free-text description of the business reason for the most recent change to this safety stock policy (e.g., 'Seasonal uplift for Q4 holiday demand', 'Supplier lead time increase', 'Post-launch NPD recalibration'). Required for audit and S&OP governance.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; ISO 9001

description: Timestamp when this safety stock policy record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning

description: Number of forward-looking days of demand used in the safety stock calculation. Defines the planning horizon over which demand variability is measured for statistical safety stock methods.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Standard deviation or coefficient of variation of demand used in statistical safety stock calculations. Derived from historical demand sensing data in SAP IBP. Higher values result in larger safety stock buffers.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning; APICS CPIM

description: Date from which this safety stock policy becomes binding and governs replenishment decisions for the SKU-location combination. Aligns with IBP/S&OP planning cycle activation.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; ISO 22000 Supply Chain Management

description: Date on which this safety stock policy ceases to be effective. Null indicates an open-ended policy with no planned expiration. Used to manage seasonal or promotional policy windows.

business_glossary_term: SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) Policy ID
description: Source system identifier for this safety stock policy record in SAP IBP (Integrated Business Planning). Used for cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the SAP IBP operational system.

Indicates whether this safety stock policy applies to a new product introduction (NPD/NPI). New products typically use manual or days-of-supply methods due to absence of historical demand data. Triggers enhanced monitoring during launch phase.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; NPD; PLM

description: Indicates whether this policy includes a promotional uplift buffer to support planned trade promotions (TPM/TPO events). When True, additional safety stock is pre-positioned at the DC ahead of promotional periods.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning; TPM

description: Indicates whether this safety stock policy incorporates seasonal demand adjustments. When True, the safety stock quantity and days of supply parameters are adjusted based on seasonal demand patterns from the demand planning system.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning

business_glossary_term: JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning Policy ID
description: Source system identifier for this safety stock policy record in JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning. Populated when JDA is the system of record for replenishment planning at the DC network level.

Date on which this safety stock policy was last reviewed by the supply planning team. Supports periodic review cadence governance within the IBP/S&OP cycle. Policies not reviewed within the review cycle may be flagged for recalibration.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Average replenishment lead time in days from order placement to receipt at the DC location. Used in reorder point and statistical safety stock calculations. Sourced from supplier SLA agreements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SLA

description: Standard deviation of replenishment lead time in days. Used in statistical safety stock calculations to account for supply uncertainty. Higher variability increases the required safety stock buffer.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; APICS CPIM

description: Maximum allowable on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the location. Defines the upper bound of the min/max replenishment band. Replenishment orders are sized to bring inventory up to this level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Minimum allowable on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the location. Defines the lower bound of the min/max replenishment band. Replenishment is triggered when inventory falls to or below this level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Minimum Order Quantity constraint applied to replenishment orders for this SKU-location policy. Sourced from supplier agreements or manufacturing run minimums. Replenishment orders cannot be placed below this quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing; Oracle Cloud Procurement

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this safety stock policy. Drives the supply planning review calendar and ensures policies remain calibrated to current demand and supply conditions.

Supply chain planning segment classification for the SKU-location combination. Determines the applicable replenishment and safety stock strategy. 'make_to_stock' is most common for CPG/food and beverage finished goods.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; APICS CPIM

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this safety stock policy record across systems (e.g., SAP IBP, JDA/Blue Yonder). Used for cross-system reconciliation and EDI references.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Human-readable name describing the safety stock policy, typically referencing the SKU, location, or planning segment it governs (e.g., 'RTD Beverage - DC East - Statistical SS').

Current lifecycle status of the safety stock policy. 'active' means the policy is live and governing replenishment; 'draft' means under configuration; 'pending_review' means awaiting S&OP approval; 'suspended' means temporarily disabled.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Version number of the safety stock policy record, incremented each time the policy parameters are updated. Supports policy change history tracking and audit trail requirements within the supply planning governance framework.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Minimum acceptable remaining shelf life percentage at time of receipt or shipment from the DC. Ensures safety stock inventory meets customer and retailer freshness requirements. Expressed as a percentage of total shelf life (e.g., 66.67 means at least 2/3 of shelf life must remain).
reference: FDA FSMA; GFSI; ISO 22000; Codex Alimentarius; GMP

description: Inventory level at which a replenishment order is triggered for the SKU at the specified location. Calculated as safety stock plus expected demand during replenishment lead time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; APICS CPIM

description: Standard replenishment order quantity triggered when the reorder point is reached. May be constrained by MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) or pallet/layer rounding rules from the supplier or DC network.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; SAP IBP Inventory Optimization

description: Frequency in days at which inventory position is reviewed and replenishment decisions are made. Relevant for periodic review systems. Impacts the safety stock calculation for periodic review models.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Inventory rotation rule applied at the DC location for this SKU. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for ambient products; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is mandatory for perishable, chilled, and frozen food and beverage products to minimize waste and ensure food safety compliance.
reference: FDA FSMA; GFSI; ISO 22000; HACCP; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Safety stock expressed as number of days of forward demand coverage. Used when calculation_method is 'days_of_supply'. Provides a demand-relative buffer measure aligned with FIFO/FEFO rotation policies.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Statistical safety factor (z-score) corresponding to the target service level. Applied to demand and lead time variability to compute the safety stock quantity in statistical calculation methods. Derived from the normal distribution at the specified service level target.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; APICS CPIM

description: The calculated or manually set safety stock quantity (in base unit of measure) that must be maintained as a buffer to protect against demand variability and supply uncertainty. Principal quantitative fact of this policy record.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; SAP S/4HANA MM MRP; APICS CPIM

description: Target service level (cycle service level or fill rate) expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.5) used in statistical safety stock calculations. Drives the safety factor (z-score) applied to demand variability. Directly impacts OTIF performance.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; APICS CPIM; ISO 22000

description: Total shelf life of the SKU in days from manufacture date. Used in FEFO rotation policy enforcement and safety stock sizing to ensure buffer inventory does not expire before consumption. Critical for perishable food and beverage products.
reference: FDA FSMA; USDA; ISO 22000; GFSI; Codex Alimentarius

description: Base unit of measure in which safety stock quantities, reorder points, and replenishment quantities are expressed (e.g., CS for case, EA for each, KG for kilogram). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA material master UOM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; GS1 Unit of Measure Standard; ISO 80000

description: Timestamp when this safety stock policy record was most recently modified. Used to detect changes during incremental Silver layer processing and to support S&OP change management audit trails.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning

description: XYZ classification of the SKU based on demand variability. 'X' items have stable, predictable demand; 'Y' items have moderate variability; 'Z' items have highly erratic demand. Combined with ABC classification to set differentiated safety stock policies.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Demand Planning; APICS CPIM

description: Primary key for inbound_receipt

Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Three-way match: inbound_receipt (GR) triggers AP invoice matching and payment release. Finance links receipt documents to AP invoices for GRIR clearing — a mandatory F&B procurement-to-pay control. p

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_standard_cost. Business justification: Goods receipt valuation uses standard costs; inbound_receipt is valued at the standard cost of the received material for inventory accounting. F&B finance teams reconcile receipt value to standard cos

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Goods receipt postings occur within fiscal periods; finance assigns inventory value to the correct fiscal period for period-close. posting_date is a plain date column; a proper FK to fiscal_period ena

Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Supply inbound_receipt and procurement goods_receipt represent the same physical receiving event from two domain perspectives. Linking them enables cross-domain audit trail reconciliation and FSMA tra
Reference to the inbound logistics shipment or Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) associated with this goods receipt. Enables end-to-end supply chain traceability from supplier dispatch to DC receipt.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; GS1 ASN (DESADV) Standard

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Goods receipt posting automatically generates a journal entry (inventory debit, GR/IR credit). F&B finance teams trace inbound receipts to their accounting documents for audit and period-close reconci

Foreign key linking to ingredient.lot. Business justification: Posting an inbound receipt creates or references an ingredient lot for FSMA traceability and quality management. F&B receiving processes require linking the physical receipt event to the lot record. s

Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_trace. Business justification: FSMA traceability requires linking goods receipt events to lot trace records at the point of receipt. inbound_receipt carries a denormalized batch_number; a proper FK to lot_trace enables one-up/one-d
SAP plant code (WERKS) identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center where the goods were physically received. Drives inventory posting and valuation area.

Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Line-level 3-way match requires receipt records to reference the specific PO line for quantity variance tracking, underdelivery/overdelivery tolerance checks, and invoice verification. purchase_order_

Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: 3-way match (PO-GR-Invoice) is a core AP process in F&B. inbound_receipt must reference the governing purchase_order directly for quantity variance reconciliation and payment verification. purchase_or

Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Inbound receipts occur at specific network nodes (DCs, manufacturing plants, co-packer facilities). inbound_receipt has plant_id (cross-domain to manufacturing.plant) for manufacturing plants, but net

Foreign key linking to supply.replenishment_order. Business justification: An inbound receipt is the physical goods receipt that closes out a replenishment order. inbound_receipt already links to inbound_shipment (which links to replenishment_order), but a direct FK from inb

Foreign key linking to product.shelf_life_spec. Business justification: F&B receiving operations validate inbound receipts against shelf life specs: dc_receiving_standard, rotation_rule, and remaining_shelf_life_days thresholds are defined in shelf_life_spec. FSMA 204 tra
Internal identifier for the material or finished good received (MATNR). Links to the material master for product specifications, allergen data, shelf life parameters, and storage requirements.

Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Inbound receiving process: received material is verified against the approved specification (CoA validation, shelf life, storage condition). Linking inbound_receipt to specification enables receiving

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Goods receipt posting (SAP movement type 101) creates or updates a stock position record. The inbound_receipt is the transactional document that posts inventory into stock; F&B warehouse managers requ
SAP storage location code (LGORT) within the plant where received goods are physically placed. Determines bin assignment in WMS and supports FIFO/FEFO rotation policy enforcement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.LGORT; SAP WM

description: Internal identifier for the supplier/vendor (LIFNR) from whom the materials were received. Enables supplier performance tracking, OTIF measurement, and accounts payable matching. PARTY_REFERENCE category.

Supplier-transmitted Advanced Shipping Notice number received via EDI (856 transaction set) prior to physical delivery. Used to pre-receive inventory, schedule dock appointments, and validate shipment contents against PO.
reference: GS1 DESADV; ANSI X12 856 EDI; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Carrier-issued bill of lading number for the inbound shipment. Serves as the legal contract of carriage and is required for freight claims, customs clearance, and 3PL reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; US UCC Article 7; INCOTERMS 2020

description: Reference number of the supplier-provided Certificate of Analysis accompanying the shipment. Documents quality test results (microbiological, chemical, physical) for the received batch. Required for GFSI, SQF, and HACCP compliance.
reference: TraceGains Supplier Compliance; GFSI; SQF; HACCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 117

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the received material was manufactured or grown. Required for customs declarations, USDA country-of-origin labeling (COOL), and import compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.HERBL; USDA COOL; FDA Import Regulations; Codex Alimentarius

description: Timestamp when the goods receipt record was first created in the source system (SAP CPUDT + CPUTM). Used for audit trail and data lineage. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the valuation and receipt value amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency operations across global DC network.
Supplier-provided delivery note or packing slip number (LIEFN) accompanying the inbound shipment. Used for three-way match (PO, GR, invoice) and ASN reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MKPF.XBLNR; GS1 ASN Standard

description: The date on the physical delivery document or packing slip (BLDAT) as provided by the supplier. May differ from posting_date when receipts are backdated or processed with delay.

Expiration or best-before date of the received batch. Governs FEFO inventory rotation, shelf life compliance, and eligibility for distribution. Mandatory for perishable food and beverage products per FDA and EFSA regulations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master — MHDRZ; FDA 21 CFR Part 101; EU Reg 1169/2011; EFSA

description: Free-text notes entered by the receiving clerk at time of goods receipt. Captures exceptions, damage observations, temperature deviations, or other relevant receiving conditions not captured in structured fields.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MKPF.BKTXT; GMP Documentation

description: INCOTERMS code from the purchase order defining the point of title transfer and risk for the inbound shipment (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP, EXW). Determines when inventory ownership transfers to Food Beverage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKKO.INCO1; ICC INCOTERMS 2020

description: Indicates whether the received goods are consignment stock (supplier-owned inventory held at Food Beverage's facility). True = consignment; False = standard purchased stock. Affects accounts payable timing and inventory ownership.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Consignment Processing; IAS 2

description: Indicates whether this goods receipt represents a return delivery from a customer or a reversal of a prior outbound delivery being returned to stock. True = return; False = standard inbound receipt.
Date on which the received batch was manufactured by the supplier or co-packer. Used for shelf life calculation, FEFO rotation policy, and food safety compliance documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Master; FDA 21 CFR Part 117; FSMA

description: Fiscal year (MJAHR) associated with the SAP material document number. Required in combination with material_document_number to uniquely identify the document in SAP S/4HANA.

SAP MM movement type code (BWART) classifying the nature of the goods movement (e.g., 101 = GR for PO, 103 = GR into blocked stock, 105 = GR from blocked stock, 501 = receipt without PO). Drives inventory account determination and stock type posting.

Original quantity ordered on the purchase order line item. Compared against received_qty to calculate receipt variance and identify short-shipments or over-deliveries for supplier performance management.

The accounting posting date (BUDAT) on which the goods receipt is recorded in SAP, triggering inventory update and accounts payable accrual. Determines the fiscal period for financial reporting. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MKPF.BUDAT; IAS 2 Inventories

description: SAP QM inspection lot number (PRUEFLOS) created automatically upon goods receipt for materials subject to quality inspection. Links the receipt to the quality inspection results and usage decision in Veeva Vault or SAP QM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM — QMEL; Veeva Vault QMS; ISO 22000; FSSC 22000

description: Outcome of the quality inspection usage decision for the received batch. 'accepted' = released to unrestricted stock; 'rejected' = returned to vendor or destroyed; 'conditional_release' = released with deviations noted; 'pending' = inspection in progress; 'waived' = skip-lot or pre-approved supplier.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM; Veeva Vault QMS; ISO 22000; GFSI; SQF

description: Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt transaction. 'posted' = inventory updated; 'reversed' = full reversal posted; 'blocked' = held pending quality or compliance review; 'in_quality_inspection' = under QM usage decision; 'partially_reversed' = partial quantity reversed; 'cancelled' = voided before posting. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: posted|reversed|blocked|in_quality_inspection|partially_reversed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Movement Type (BWART); FSMA 204

description: Precise date and time when the goods were physically received and scanned at the dock door or receiving bay. Used for OTIF calculation, lead time analysis, and dock scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; GS1 EPCIS Standard

description: Actual quantity of material physically received and posted to inventory (MENGE). Expressed in the base unit of measure. Used for inventory reconciliation, fill rate calculation, and OTIF measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.MENGE; GS1 GPC

description: Classification of the receiving location type. Distinguishes between manufacturing plants, distribution centers (DC), co-packer facilities, 3PL warehouses, and cross-dock points. Drives putaway rules and inventory ownership.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Configuration; Supply Chain Network Design

description: Number of days remaining between the receipt posting date and the expiration date at time of receipt. Used to enforce minimum remaining shelf life (MRSL) thresholds for customer acceptance and distribution eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management; GS1 Shelf Life Standards; GFSI

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this goods receipt record was ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration auditing.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: SAP stock type (INSMK) into which the received quantity is posted. 'unrestricted' = available for use/sale; 'quality_inspection' = under QM hold; 'blocked' = not available; 'restricted' = batch-restricted; 'returns' = returned goods stock. Governs FIFO/FEFO rotation eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.INSMK; HACCP; GMP

description: Total inventory value posted for this receipt (valuation_price × received_qty) in local currency. Drives the accounts payable accrual entry and inventory balance update in SAP FI/CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.DMBTR; IAS 2; IFRS 15

description: Base unit of measure (MEINS) in which the received quantity is expressed (e.g., KG, L, EA, CS, PAL). Aligns with the material master base UOM for inventory valuation and planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.MEINS; UN/CEFACT Rec 20

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this goods receipt record in the source system. Supports change detection in the Silver Layer ETL pipeline and audit compliance. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.

Unit price at which the received material is valuated in inventory (DMBTR/MENGE). Used for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, and accounts payable accrual. Reflects moving average price or standard price per material master. MONETARY_TRIPLET category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — MSEG.DMBTR; IAS 2 Inventories

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the inbound shipment record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete inbound logistics event with a full lifecycle from ASN through goods receipt and putaway.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module — Goods Receipt (MIGO/MIGO_GR)

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Three-way match: inbound shipment (goods receipt) is matched against the AP invoice for payment processing. F&B procurement finance teams link GR documents to AP invoices for invoice verification; inv

Foreign key linking to ingredient.approved_supplier. Business justification: Goods receipt verification requires confirming each inbound shipment originates from an approved supplier-ingredient combination. F&B quality and FSMA compliance mandate this check at receiving. A QA

Reference to the transportation carrier or 3PL partner responsible for the inbound shipment leg. Used for carrier performance tracking, OTIF (On Time In Full) measurement, and freight cost allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Transportation (LT01); JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.compliance_document. Business justification: FSMA and GFSI require linking each inbound shipment to its governing compliance document (COA, food safety cert). certificate_of_analysis_ref is a denormalized text reference. A proper FK enables docu
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocate inbound logistics expenses to the responsible cost center for expense tracking.

Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Inbound shipments arrive at a specific network node (DC, manufacturing plant, co-packer). inbound_shipment.storage_location_code is a more granular bin/rack-level reference and is kept. Adding destina

Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Inbound shipments are directed to a specific storage location for putaway (WMS directed putaway process). inbound_shipment carries a denormalized storage_location_code; replacing it with a proper FK e

Foreign key linking to distribution.shipment. Business justification: Inbound supplier shipments (supply domain) must be reconciled with distribution.shipment records for the same physical movement. F&B DCs use this link for OTIF reconciliation, goods receipt matching,

Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Inbound shipments must be tied to the registered facility of the supplier for FDA/USDA import compliance tracking.

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Inbound shipments are posted to fiscal periods for goods receipt accounting and COGS recognition. Finance requires shipment-to-period linkage for inventory valuation and period-close; goods_receipt_ti
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Post freight and handling charges of inbound shipments to the designated GL account.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.import_export_permit. Business justification: Cross-border inbound shipments require a valid import permit for customs clearance and FDA prior notice compliance. Linking each shipment to its governing import/export permit is a standard customs an
Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: INBOUND_SHIPMENT quality inspection results must be linked to INSPECTION_LOT for compliance release and traceability reports.

Foreign key linking to supply.lane. Business justification: Inbound shipments travel from suppliers/co-packers to DCs/plants along defined transportation lanes. Linking inbound_shipment to lane enables lane-level inbound performance tracking (transit time actu
Reference to the receiving plant or Distribution Center (DC) in the supply network where the inbound shipment is destined. Used for network design analytics and inventory update triggering.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Plant/Storage Location (OX10); Supply Chain Network Design

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Inbound shipments must be matched to specific PO lines for line-level OTIF measurement, delivery performance tracking, and ASN-to-PO-line reconciliation — standard F&B inbound logistics process. inbou

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Inbound shipments are allocated to profit centers for product-line COGS reporting. F&B finance teams attribute inbound freight and material costs to the correct profit center for brand/product-line P&
Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) must be tied to the originating purchase order for traceability and compliance reporting.

Foreign key linking to ingredient.raw_material. Business justification: Inbound shipments of raw materials must reference the raw material master for receiving, quality inspection scheduling, allergen verification, and FSMA traceability. inbound_shipment has sku_id for fi

Reference to the material or Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) being received. Links to the product/material master for ingredient, packaging, or finished goods identification. Supports RESOURCE_REFERENCE for downstream line-level analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Material Master (MM01); GS1 GTIN Standard

description: Reference to the supplier, co-packer, or inter-company transfer source party in the master data. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for carrier performance tracking and supplier compliance reporting via TraceGains.
