description: Human-readable descriptive name for the capacity constraint (e.g., 'Line 3 Daily Throughput Cap', 'DC Chicago Pallet Positions'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this capacity constraint record originated. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation across planning systems. 'sap_ibp' = SAP Integrated Business Planning; 'jda_blue_yonder' = JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; 'aveva_mes' = Aveva/Wonderware MES; 'manual' = planner-entered; 'erp' = SAP S/4HANA or Oracle Cloud ERP.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder; Aveva/Wonderware MES; SAP S/4HANA

description: Current lifecycle status of the capacity constraint record. 'active' means the constraint is live and enforced in planning runs; 'inactive' means it has been retired or superseded; 'pending' means it is defined but not yet effective; 'suspended' means temporarily bypassed. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Indicates whether the capacity constraint is a hard or soft limit in supply planning. 'hard' constraints cannot be violated by the planning engine (e.g., physical line speed, dock door count); 'soft' constraints can be overridden with a penalty cost or planner approval (e.g., preferred utilization ceiling, labor overtime threshold).
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Timestamp when this capacity constraint record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 8601; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Governance

description: Number of dock doors available at the DC for inbound and/or outbound operations within the capacity bucket. DC-specific metric used to plan ASN (Advanced Shipping Notice) scheduling, carrier appointments, and DSD (Direct Store Delivery) operations. Null when resource_type is not 'dock_door' or 'dc_throughput'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; ASN Standards

description: Number of dock doors actually scheduled or utilized within the capacity bucket period. Used alongside dock_doors_available to compute dock door utilization rate and identify scheduling bottlenecks at the DC.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Net usable capacity after applying OEE, planned downtime, changeover time, and other efficiency factors to the theoretical available_capacity. Represents the realistic throughput ceiling used in constrained supply planning runs. Expressed in capacity_uom.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; SAP S/4HANA PP-CRP; Aveva/Wonderware MES

description: Date after which this capacity constraint record is no longer active and is excluded from planning runs. Null for open-ended constraints. Used to manage time-bound constraints such as seasonal capacity limits or temporary equipment outages.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; ISO 8601

description: Date from which this capacity constraint record becomes effective and is enforced in planning runs. Enables forward-looking constraint management and scenario planning for known future capacity changes (e.g., planned line shutdowns, DC expansions).
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; ISO 8601

description: Total labor hours available for production or DC operations within the capacity bucket, accounting for scheduled shifts, headcount, and planned absences. Used in labor capacity planning and S&OP cycles. Null when resource_type is not 'labor'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Workday HCM; SAP IBP

description: Actual or planned labor hours consumed within the capacity bucket period. Used to assess workforce loading against available labor hours and identify overtime or underutilization scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Workday HCM; SAP IBP

description: Date on which this capacity constraint record was last reviewed and validated by a supply planner or capacity manager. Used to ensure constraint parameters remain current and aligned with operational reality across S&OP cycles.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; S&OP Best Practices

description: Target OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) percentage for the production line or equipment resource within this capacity bucket. OEE = Availability × Performance × Quality. Used to set realistic effective capacity expectations in finite scheduling and SAP IBP planning runs. Applicable only when resource_type is 'production_line'.
reference: Aveva/Wonderware MES Performance Analytics; OEE Industry Standard; SAP IBP

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Risk Flag
description: Indicates whether this capacity constraint poses a risk to OTIF (On Time In Full) delivery performance (True = OTIF risk present). Set when the constraint is projected to cause order fulfillment shortfalls within the bucket period. Used to prioritize constraint resolution and escalation.
reference: SAP IBP Supply Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; OTIF Industry Standard

description: Recommended or approved action to resolve a capacity overload when bottleneck_flag is True. 'co_pack' triggers co-packing or toll manufacturing evaluation; '3pl_overflow' routes excess volume to a 3PL partner; 'demand_defer' defers lower-priority demand; 'interplant_transfer' reallocates production to another plant; 'none' means no action required.
reference: SAP IBP Supply Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Total number of pallet storage positions available at the DC or warehouse for this capacity bucket. DC-specific metric used in warehouse capacity management and network design. Null when resource_type is not 'storage' or 'dc_throughput'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Warehouse Management; WMS Standards

description: Hours of scheduled downtime within the capacity bucket for maintenance, sanitation (GMP/HACCP compliance), changeovers, or regulatory inspections. Reduces effective_capacity from available_capacity. Critical for food and beverage manufacturing where sanitation windows are mandated by GMP and FSMA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Aveva/Wonderware MES; FDA FSMA; GMP; HACCP

description: Free-text field for supply planners to document context, assumptions, or exceptions related to this capacity constraint (e.g., 'Line 3 down for allergen deep clean week of 2024-03-11', 'DC Chicago dock expansion in progress'). Supports S&OP cycle documentation and audit trail.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder; S&OP Best Practices

description: Identifier for the SAP IBP or JDA planning version or scenario to which this capacity constraint record belongs (e.g., 'BASELINE', 'SCENARIO_A', 'CONSENSUS'). Enables multi-scenario capacity analysis and S&OP cycle management.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Indicates whether this capacity constraint is driven by a regulatory hold or compliance requirement (True = regulatory-driven constraint). Examples include FDA FSMA inspection holds, USDA audit shutdowns, or GMP sanitation mandates that reduce available production capacity.
reference: FDA FSMA; USDA; GMP; HACCP; GFSI

description: Categorizes the type of supply chain resource to which this capacity constraint applies. Drives how the constraint is interpreted in finite capacity planning: production_line (manufacturing throughput), dc_throughput (Distribution Center inbound/outbound volume), transportation_lane (carrier/lane capacity), dock_door (DC dock door availability), labor (workforce hours), storage (pallet positions or cubic volume). Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Quantity of safety stock that cannot be replenished due to this capacity constraint within the bucket period, expressed in capacity_uom. Used in inventory optimization to quantify the risk exposure created by constrained capacity and inform safety stock parameter adjustments in SAP IBP.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Timestamp when this capacity constraint record was last modified. Used to detect changes between planning cycles and support incremental Silver layer processing and audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 8601; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Governance

description: Maximum acceptable utilization percentage for this resource before the constraint is flagged as a bottleneck or overload. Typically set below 100% to preserve buffer capacity (e.g., 85% for production lines to allow for changeovers and maintenance, 90% for DC throughput). Used in SAP IBP and JDA planning to trigger alerts and rebalancing.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; OEE Standards

description: Ratio of utilized_capacity to available_capacity expressed as a percentage (0–100). Calculated field stored for planning visibility: utilization_pct = (utilized_capacity / available_capacity) * 100. Used to identify bottleneck resources and trigger network rebalancing decisions. Values above the utilization_ceiling_pct indicate an overload condition.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) Standards

description: Actual or planned capacity consumed within the bucket period, expressed in the same unit of measure as available_capacity (capacity_uom). Used to compute utilization rate and identify bottlenecks. In constrained planning runs, this reflects the sum of all planned orders or shipments assigned to this resource.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Management; SAP S/4HANA PP-CRP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the transportation lane record. Primary key for the lane entity.
Reference to the destination network node (e.g., Distribution Center (DC), customer distribution center, retail store, or 3PL facility) to which shipments are delivered on this lane.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.import_export_permit. Business justification: Cross-border transport lanes require a valid import/export permit to operate legally. Lane activation, freight tendering, and trade compliance reviews depend on permit validity. The lane already carri

Reference to the secondary or backup carrier assigned to this lane, activated when the primary carrier is unavailable or capacity-constrained. Supports supply chain resilience and continuity planning.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Reference to the primary carrier assigned to this transportation lane for standard replenishment and distribution movements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Reference to the origin network node (e.g., manufacturing plant, Distribution Center (DC), supplier facility, or 3PL warehouse) from which shipments depart on this lane.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Estimated CO2 equivalent emissions per unit distance (kg CO2e per mile) for this lane based on freight mode and carrier fleet profile. Supports sustainability reporting and EPA environmental compliance.
reference: EPA Environmental Compliance; ISO 14064; GHG Protocol

description: Date from which the carrier contract and associated freight rates for this lane become effective. Used for rate validity checks during shipment planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Date on which the carrier contract and associated freight rates for this lane expire. Triggers carrier bid management and contract renewal workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Reference number or identifier of the freight contract or carrier rate agreement governing this lane. Links to the carrier contract management system for rate validation and audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Indicates whether this lane crosses an international border, triggering customs documentation, import/export compliance, and regulatory reporting requirements (e.g., FDA Prior Notice for food imports).
reference: FDA FSMA; Codex Alimentarius Commission; EFSA

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all freight cost fields on this lane (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency network operations.
Comma-separated list of days of the week on which deliveries are accepted at the destination node for this lane (e.g., 'Tue,Thu,Sat'). Used for appointment scheduling and receiving window management.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination node's country (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Required for cross-border compliance, import duties, and regulatory reporting.
Calculated or contracted road/rail distance between origin and destination nodes in miles. Used for freight cost estimation, carrier rate negotiation, and carbon emissions modeling.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; EPA Environmental Compliance

description: Indicates whether this lane is used for Direct Store Delivery (DSD), where product is shipped directly from a manufacturing plant or DC to a retail store, bypassing the customer's distribution center.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Date from which this transportation lane becomes operationally active and available for replenishment routing and shipment planning.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP IBP

description: Date on which this transportation lane is scheduled to become inactive or is retired from the network. Nullable for open-ended lanes.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP IBP

description: Contracted flat-rate freight cost per full truckload (TL) or equivalent load unit on this lane. Applicable primarily for TL and dedicated lanes where per-load pricing is negotiated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Contracted or benchmark freight cost per mile for this lane, used in carrier bid management, rate benchmarking, and transportation cost modeling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Standard contracted freight cost per unit (case or pallet) shipped on this lane, expressed in the operating currency. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) allocation, transportation cost modeling, and network optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Transportation mode used on this lane. TL = Truckload, LTL = Less-Than-Truckload, intermodal = combined rail/truck, parcel = small package carrier, rail = dedicated rail, ocean = sea freight. Drives freight cost modeling and carrier selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TL|LTL|intermodal|parcel|rail|ocean|air — promote to reference product]
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Unit of measure used for freight cost and capacity calculations on this lane (e.g., pallet, case, lb, kg, cwt). Aligns with carrier contract terms and SAP S/4HANA MM configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Current fuel surcharge percentage applied on top of the base freight rate for this lane, expressed as a percentage. Updated periodically per carrier contract terms and fuel index benchmarks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Indicates whether this lane is certified and approved for the transport of hazardous materials (e.g., cleaning chemicals, CO2 cartridges). Drives carrier certification requirements and regulatory compliance checks.
Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the lane, used in carrier bid management, freight contracts, and Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) communications. Unique across the network.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; EDI X12 Standard

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the lane, typically combining origin and destination node names (e.g., 'Chicago DC to Atlanta DC'). Used in reporting and network design tools.

Current operational lifecycle status of the lane. 'active' lanes are available for replenishment routing; 'inactive' lanes are retired; 'pending' lanes are under carrier negotiation; 'suspended' lanes are temporarily halted.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP IBP

description: Classification of the lane's operational role. 'primary' is the standard routing; 'backup' is an alternate route activated when primary is unavailable; 'spot' is used for ad-hoc shipments; 'dedicated' is a contracted exclusive lane.
Date on which this lane record was last reviewed and validated by the transportation or supply chain planning team. Supports lane master data governance and periodic network design reviews.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP IBP

description: Total replenishment lead time in days for this lane, including order processing, pick/pack, and transit time. Distinct from transit_time_days as it captures the full end-to-end cycle used in demand planning and inventory optimization.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Maximum shipment capacity per load on this lane, expressed in the freight unit of measure (e.g., pallets, cases, or weight). Used for load planning and carrier capacity management.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) required to activate a shipment on this lane, expressed in the freight unit of measure. Enforced during replenishment signal generation in SAP IBP.

Geographic or operational region classification for this lane (e.g., 'Northeast', 'Southeast', 'Midwest', 'West Coast', 'International'). Used for network design segmentation and regional distribution analytics.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP IBP

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or exceptions relevant to this lane (e.g., seasonal restrictions, bridge weight limits, port congestion advisories).
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the origin node's country (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Required for cross-border compliance, customs documentation, and trade lane analytics.
reference: ISO 3166-1; FDA FSMA; Codex Alimentarius Commission

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Target Percentage
description: Contracted On Time In Full (OTIF) performance target percentage for this lane, used to measure carrier compliance and supply chain reliability. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 98.50).
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP IBP

description: Comma-separated list of days of the week on which carrier pickups are available at the origin node for this lane (e.g., 'Mon,Wed,Fri'). Used for replenishment scheduling and order cut-off management.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Contracted service level for this lane defining delivery speed and reliability commitment (e.g., standard, expedited, overnight, economy). Linked to On Time In Full (OTIF) and Service Level Agreement (SLA) performance targets.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP IBP

description: Operational system of record from which this lane record originates (e.g., SAP Transportation Management, JDA/Blue Yonder TMS, Oracle SCM, or manually maintained). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; Oracle Cloud ERP

description: Temperature-controlled handling requirement for this lane, critical for food safety compliance. 'ambient' = dry goods; 'chilled' = refrigerated (2-8°C); 'frozen' = frozen (-18°C or below); 'controlled' = specific temperature range per product specification. Aligns with FSMA and HACCP requirements.
Indicates whether this lane is operated by a Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider rather than a company-owned or dedicated carrier. Drives 3PL performance management and cost allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Standard transit time in calendar days from origin departure to destination receipt for this lane. Used in replenishment lead time calculation, safety stock parameterization, and SAP IBP supply planning.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Standard deviation or average variability in transit time in days for this lane, derived from historical shipment performance. Used in safety stock calculation and inventory optimization in SAP IBP.
reference: SAP IBP; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each physical node in the supply chain network. Primary key for the network_node master record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; SAP IBP Network Design

description: Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Logistics network mapping ties nodes that are physical DCs to the DC master for routing, capacity planning, and compliance reporting.

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Network nodes belong to legal entities (company codes) for statutory reporting, intercompany transfers, and tax compliance. F&B multinationals must know which legal entity owns each node for transfer

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each network node (DC, plant, 3PL) maps to a cost center for overhead allocation and facility cost reporting. F&B finance teams assign cost centers to distribution nodes for P&L reporting and facility

Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Every food-handling network node (DC, warehouse, co-packer) must hold an FDA establishment registration. The denormalized fda_facility_registration_number on network_node should be replaced with a pro

Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: In global F&B, each DC, plant, or co-packer node is assigned to a market for supply network design, market allocation, and regulatory reporting. Market assignment drives service_level_target_pct and o

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Network nodes are assigned to profit centers for product-line and geographic P&L reporting. F&B companies map DCs and plants to profit centers for contribution margin analysis; cost_center alone does

Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: DC-to-territory alignment is a fundamental F&B supply network design decision — each distribution center serves defined sales territories. Network design and territory coverage analysis require this l
Primary street address of the supply chain node facility. Used for carrier routing, ASN generation, and regulatory compliance reporting (FDA FSMA facility registration).
reference: FDA FSMA Facility Registration; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Secondary address detail (suite, building, dock number) for the supply chain node. Supports precise carrier delivery instructions and ASN routing.
reference: FDA FSMA Facility Registration; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: City in which the supply chain node is located. Used in network design, lane definition, and geographic reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Indicates whether this node has co-packing or toll manufacturing capability — performing secondary packaging, kitting, or assembly operations on behalf of the brand. Relevant for NPD and promotional pack configurations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Aveva/Wonderware MES; Co-Packing; Toll Manufacturing

description: Number of pallet positions dedicated to temperature-controlled (chilled or frozen) storage. Used for dairy, beverage, and perishable product inventory planning and FEFO compliance.
reference: FDA FSMA; HACCP; SAP IBP Inventory Optimization

description: Date on which the supply chain node became operationally active. Used in asset lifecycle management, network design history, and regulatory compliance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Asset Management; Oracle Cloud Financials

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the node is located (e.g., 'USA', 'CAN', 'GBR'). Drives regulatory compliance framework selection (FDA, EFSA, USDA) and customs/trade documentation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; FDA; EFSA; USDA; Codex Alimentarius

description: Indicates whether this node supports cross-docking operations — transferring inbound shipments directly to outbound without storage. Reduces handling costs and lead times for high-velocity SKUs.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Indicates whether this node is a licensed customs bonded warehouse, allowing storage of imported goods before customs duties are paid. Relevant for international supply chain nodes and import/export operations.
reference: US Customs and Border Protection (CBP); Oracle Cloud Supply Chain

description: Date on which the supply chain node was or is planned to be decommissioned. Nullable for active nodes. Used in network design planning, lease management, and IBP scenario modeling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Asset Management; SAP IBP Network Design

description: Number of loading/unloading dock doors at the facility. Constrains inbound and outbound throughput scheduling and carrier appointment management.

Indicates whether this node supports Direct Store Delivery (DSD) operations — direct shipment from the node to retail store locations bypassing intermediate DCs. Drives route planning and trade promotion execution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; DSD

description: SAP S/4HANA or Oracle ERP plant/organization code corresponding to this network node. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the supply chain network model and ERP master data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant; Oracle Cloud Supply Chain Organization

description: Total usable floor area of the node facility in square feet. Supports capacity planning, lease management, and facility benchmarking.
reference: Oracle Cloud Financials; SAP S/4HANA RE-FX

description: Indicates whether the node holds a current Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) certification. Required for manufacturing and co-packing nodes under FDA, USDA, and GFSI compliance frameworks.
reference: FDA GMP 21 CFR Part 110/117; GFSI; ISO 22000; FSSC 22000

description: SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) location identifier for this node. Enables direct linkage between the network node master and IBP demand sensing, inventory optimization, and S&OP planning models.
reference: SAP IBP Integrated Business Planning; SAP IBP Demand Sensing

description: Decimal latitude coordinate of the node facility. Used in network optimization models, transportation lane distance calculations, and geographic visualization in IBP.
reference: SAP IBP Network Design; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Date on which the facility lease expires for leased or 3PL-operated nodes. Drives network design risk assessment, contract renewal planning, and financial forecasting in Oracle Cloud Financials.
reference: Oracle Cloud Financials; SAP S/4HANA RE-FX; IFRS 16

description: Decimal longitude coordinate of the node facility. Used in network optimization models, transportation lane distance calculations, and geographic visualization in IBP.
reference: SAP IBP Network Design; JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management

description: Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) in pallet units for inbound replenishment orders to this node. Enforced during replenishment signal generation in SAP IBP and JDA to avoid sub-optimal shipments.
reference: SAP IBP Supply Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; MOQ

description: Name of the Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider operating this node, if applicable. Used in SLA governance, contract management, and performance scorecarding.
reference: Oracle Cloud Procurement; SAP S/4HANA MM; 3PL

description: Hierarchical tier of the node within the distribution network (e.g., national DC feeds regional DCs, which feed forward DCs). Determines replenishment flow, safety stock echelon, and FIFO/FEFO rotation policy assignment.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the supply chain node across systems (e.g., SAP plant code, DC code, 3PL site code). Used in EDI transactions, ASN documents, and IBP network models.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Code; JDA/Blue Yonder Network Node; EDI X12

description: Human-readable name of the supply chain node (e.g., 'Chicago Regional DC', 'Atlanta Co-Pack Facility'). Used in reporting, planograms, and network design documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Current lifecycle status of the supply chain node. Controls whether the node is eligible for demand planning, replenishment signal generation, and network design optimization in SAP IBP and JDA.
reference: SAP IBP Network Design; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Classification of the supply chain node by its primary operational function. Drives network design rules, safety stock policy assignment, and lane definitions in IBP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing_plant|distribution_center|3pl_warehouse|co_packer|cross_dock|forward_dc — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IBP Network Design; JDA/Blue Yonder Network Modeling

description: Standard daily operating end time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) for the node. Used in carrier appointment scheduling, outbound dispatch windows, and transportation management.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Standard daily operating start time (HH:MM, 24-hour format) for the node. Used in carrier appointment scheduling, inbound receiving windows, and transportation management.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA WM

description: Describes the ownership and operational arrangement for the node — whether the facility is company-owned, leased, operated by a 3PL partner, or used for toll manufacturing or co-packing. Drives cost allocation (COGS), SLA governance, and contract management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; Oracle Cloud Procurement

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Target Percentage
description: On Time In Full (OTIF) delivery performance target percentage for shipments originating from this node. Used in SLA governance, carrier scorecards, and supply chain KPI reporting.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; OTIF; SLA

description: Maximum number of pallet positions available at the node for storage. Principal capacity metric used in inventory optimization, safety stock policy assignment, and network design in SAP IBP.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; WMS

description: Postal or ZIP code of the supply chain node. Used for carrier zone determination, freight cost calculation, and geographic clustering in network design.
reference: JDA/Blue Yonder Transportation Management; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Standard number of days required to replenish inventory at this node from its upstream supply source. Used in safety stock calculations, demand sensing, and IBP supply-demand balancing.
reference: SAP IBP Demand Sensing; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Inventory rotation method enforced at this node — First In First Out (FIFO), First Expired First Out (FEFO), or Last In First Out (LIFO). Drives WMS pick sequencing and shelf life compliance for food safety.
reference: FDA FSMA; HACCP; GMP; SAP S/4HANA WM; ISO 22000

description: Code referencing the safety stock calculation policy assigned to this node (e.g., days-of-supply, statistical, min-max). Drives inventory optimization parameters in SAP IBP and JDA demand/supply planning.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning

description: Target fill rate or case fill rate percentage (e.g., 98.5) for outbound order fulfillment from this node. Drives safety stock policy and OTIF performance measurement.
reference: SAP IBP Inventory Optimization; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; OTIF; SLA

description: ISO 3166-2 state or province code where the node is located (e.g., 'IL', 'CA', 'ON'). Used for tax jurisdiction, regulatory compliance, and transportation lane modeling.
reference: ISO 3166-2; FDA FSMA; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Temperature-controlled storage capability of the node (ambient, chilled, frozen, or multi-temp). Critical for food safety compliance (HACCP, FSMA), shelf life management, and FEFO rotation policy assignment.
reference: FDA FSMA; HACCP; GFSI; ISO 22000; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Maximum number of pallets the node can receive, process, and ship per day. Used in capacity planning, OTIF modeling, and supply-demand balancing in JDA and SAP IBP.
reference: SAP IBP Capacity Planning; JDA/Blue Yonder Supply Planning; OEE

description: IANA time zone identifier for the node location (e.g., 'America/Chicago', 'Europe/London'). Used for scheduling inbound/outbound appointments, production shifts, and EDI transaction timestamps.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; SAP S/4HANA; JDA/Blue Yonder TMS

description: System-generated unique identifier for the account record.

Foreign key linking to customer.bill_to_location. Business justification: Account billing address fields are duplicated; linking to bill_to_location centralizes address data and removes redundant columns.

Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Credit information is detailed in credit_profile; moving credit_rating and payment_terms to credit_profile normalizes credit data.

Foreign key linking to customer.edi_trading_partner. Business justification: EDI trading partner configuration belongs to a B2B account; adding FK enables direct association without creating a bidirectional link.

Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Preferred Supplier Assignment process links each key account to its primary supplier for contract negotiation and service level management.

business_glossary_term: Primary Supplier Establishment Registration Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Retailers and distributors must verify their primary supplier facilities hold valid FDA/USDA establishment registrations for food safety compliance, supply chain audits, and customer assurance program
Identifier of the sales territory responsible for the account.
Human‑readable name of the account (e.g., retailer name, consumer name).
External business number used to identify the account in contracts and transactions.
Current operational status of the account.
Category that defines the nature of the account holder.
Revenue range band for the account (e.g., <$1M, $1‑5M, $5‑10M, >$10M).

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Brand performance is aggregated in profit centers for P&L reporting; FK enables profit‑center level analysis.

Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Brand strategy defines a target consumer segment; FK replaces the denormalized segment name, enabling segment‑based budgeting and compliance reporting.

Comma-separated list of allergen-free claims associated with the brand (e.g., 'gluten-free, nut-free, dairy-free'). Derived from the allergen matrix managed in TraceGains. Must comply with FDA FALCPA (Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act) and FSMA requirements. Used in consumer marketing and retail planogram placement.
reference: FDA FALCPA 21 USC 343; FSMA 2011; Codex Alimentarius Allergen Standards; TraceGains Allergen Matrix

description: Total approved annual marketing investment budget allocated to the brand, expressed in the functional currency. Includes above-the-line (ATL) advertising, below-the-line (BTL) shopper marketing, digital, and trade promotion spend. Confidential financial data managed in Oracle Cloud ERP Financials and SAP CO cost centers.
reference: Oracle Cloud ERP Financials; SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting; EBITDA Reporting Framework

description: Classification of the brand within the corporate brand architecture hierarchy. 'masterbrand' = umbrella brand covering multiple product lines; 'sub_brand' = brand operating under a masterbrand; 'endorsed' = independent brand with parent brand endorsement; 'standalone' = fully independent brand; 'private_label' = retailer-owned brand manufactured by Food Beverage under co-packing or toll manufacturing arrangements.
reference: Brand Architecture Framework; Nielsen/IRI Brand Hierarchy Standards

description: Unaided or total brand awareness percentage among the target consumer segment, expressed as a value between 0.00 and 100.00. Sourced from Nielsen/IRI consumer panel studies or commissioned brand health tracking research. Key input to brand equity score calculation and marketing investment justification.
reference: Nielsen/IRI Panel Data; Codex Alimentarius Consumer Research Standards

description: Primary consumer product category to which the brand belongs (e.g., 'Snacks', 'Beverages', 'Dairy', 'Packaged Foods', 'RTD Coffee', 'RTE Cereals'). Aligns with Nielsen/IRI/SPINS syndicated data category taxonomy for market tracking and ACV/TDP reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: snacks|beverages|dairy|packaged_foods|rte_cereals|rtd_beverages|condiments|frozen_foods|confectionery|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Nielsen/IRI Category Taxonomy; SPINS Natural Channel Data; GS1 Product Classification

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the brand, used across SAP S/4HANA, Oracle ERP, and trade systems. Serves as the natural key for cross-system integration and EDI transactions. Example: 'SNKBR-001', 'BEV-RTD-02'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Brand Master; GS1 GTIN Standards

description: Official commercial name of the brand as registered and used in consumer-facing marketing, packaging, and trade communications. This is the primary human-readable identifier for the brand (e.g., 'SunCrisp', 'HydraFlow'). Must align with FTC-approved marketing claims and FDA labeling requirements.
reference: FTC Advertising Guidelines; FDA 21 CFR Part 101 Food Labeling

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual marketing budget (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Ensures correct financial reporting and multi-currency consolidation in Oracle Cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA FI.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Indicates whether the brand carries a clean label certification or commitment (no artificial colors, flavors, preservatives, or GMO ingredients). Drives consumer marketing claims, shelf placement in natural/organic sections, and SPINS natural channel data eligibility. Must be substantiated per FTC claim guidelines.
reference: FTC 16 CFR Part 255; FDA 21 CFR Part 101 Clean Label; GFSI Standards

description: Timestamp when the brand record was first created in the enterprise data platform (Databricks Silver Layer). Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management.
reference: ISO 8601 Date/Time Standard; FSMA Record-Keeping Requirements 21 CFR Part 1.337

description: Brand identifier as recorded in Salesforce CRM (Sales Cloud / Marketing Cloud). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse brand master and Salesforce campaign and consumer engagement records. Enables brand-level marketing performance attribution.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; Salesforce Marketing Cloud

description: Primary website URL for the brand's consumer-facing digital presence (e.g., 'https://www.suncrisp.com'). Used in Salesforce Marketing Cloud digital campaign management, SEO tracking, and consumer engagement analytics.
reference: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Digital Marketing; FTC Online Advertising Guidelines

description: Calendar date on which the brand was officially discontinued or withdrawn from the market. Null if the brand is currently active. Used for portfolio rationalization analysis, regulatory deregistration, and historical reporting in Nielsen/IRI market tracking.
reference: SAP PLM Lifecycle Management; FDA FSMA Product Withdrawal

description: Primary route-to-market channel through which the brand's products are sold. 'retail' = traditional grocery/mass/drug; 'foodservice' = restaurants/institutions; 'dsd' = Direct Store Delivery; 'dtc' = Direct-to-Consumer; 'ecommerce' = online retail; 'club' = club/warehouse channel. Drives trade promotion strategy (TPM/TPO) and supply chain configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Distribution Channel; JDA Blue Yonder Supply Planning; TPM Framework

description: Composite brand equity score derived from consumer research inputs including awareness, consideration, preference, and loyalty metrics. Scored on a 0-100 scale. Sourced from Nielsen/IRI brand health tracking studies and internal NPS panel data. Confidential strategic metric used in portfolio investment decisions and brand valuation.
reference: Nielsen/IRI Brand Health Tracking; ISO 10668 Brand Valuation; Salesforce Marketing Cloud NPS

description: Brand identifier as recorded in the source ERP system (SAP S/4HANA or Oracle Cloud ERP). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer brand master and operational ERP brand records. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; Oracle Cloud ERP Supply Chain

business_glossary_term: Global Food Safety Initiative (GFSI) Certification Scheme
description: GFSI-benchmarked food safety certification scheme applicable to the brand's manufacturing operations. 'sqf' = Safe Quality Food (SQF); 'brc' = British Retail Consortium Global Standard; 'fssc_22000' = Food Safety System Certification 22000; 'ifs' = International Featured Standards; 'global_gap' = GlobalG.A.P.; 'none' = no GFSI certification. Tracked in Veeva Vault and TraceGains supplier compliance.
reference: GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; FSSC 22000; SQF Code; BRC Global Standard; ISO 22000

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Plan Reference
description: Reference identifier for the HACCP plan document associated with the brand's manufacturing process, stored in Veeva Vault Document Control. HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) is a mandatory food safety management system under FDA FSMA and USDA FSIS regulations. Used for audit traceability and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; USDA FSIS HACCP Regulations 9 CFR Part 417; Codex Alimentarius HACCP Guidelines; Veeva Vault Document Control

description: Calendar date on which the brand was first commercially launched and made available to consumers in its primary market. Used for brand age calculations, anniversary marketing campaigns, and PLM lifecycle tracking. Aligns with SAP PLM product launch milestones.
reference: SAP PLM NPD Launch Management; FDA FSMA Product Registration

description: Legally registered name of the brand as filed with trademark authorities (USPTO, EUIPO, etc.). May differ from the commercial brand name. Used for regulatory filings, contract management, and IP protection tracking.
reference: USPTO Trademark Registration; EUIPO Brand Registry; FDA 21 CFR Part 101

description: Current state of the brand within its commercial lifecycle. 'active' = currently marketed and sold; 'discontinued' = no longer produced or sold; 'pipeline' = in NPD/R&D phase, not yet launched; 'divested' = sold or transferred to another entity; 'archived' = historical record retained for compliance. Drives PLM alignment and portfolio management decisions.
reference: SAP PLM Brand Lifecycle; Veeva Vault Quality Management; FDA FSMA

description: URL pointing to the master brand logo asset stored in the digital asset management (DAM) system. Used by Salesforce Marketing Cloud, packaging design teams, and agency partners for consistent brand identity application across all consumer touchpoints.
reference: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Content Builder; Brand Identity Standards

description: Net Promoter Score (NPS) for the brand, measuring consumer likelihood to recommend on a -100 to +100 scale. Sourced from consumer panel surveys and Salesforce Marketing Cloud feedback programs. Used as a leading indicator of brand health and consumer loyalty in brand equity tracking.
reference: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Service Cloud; Nielsen Panel Data; NPS Methodology (Bain & Company)

description: Indicates whether the brand holds USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification or equivalent international organic certification (e.g., EU Organic). Enables organic marketing claims on packaging and in trade communications. Tracked in TraceGains supplier compliance and Veeva Vault document control.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; EU Organic Regulation 2018/848

description: Internal business division or operating unit that owns and manages the brand (e.g., 'North America Snacks', 'Global Beverages', 'International Dairy'). Used for P&L attribution, marketing budget allocation, and organizational reporting in SAP CO and Oracle Financials.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center; Oracle Cloud ERP Financials

business_glossary_term: Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) Product Line Code
description: Reference code linking the brand to its associated product line in the PLM system (SAP PLM or equivalent). Enables traceability between brand master data and the SKU/product hierarchy managed in PLM. Used for NPD pipeline tracking and BOM management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM PLM Integration; Oracle Cloud ERP Supply Chain PLM

description: Internal strategic statement articulating the brand's unique value proposition, competitive differentiation, and reason-to-believe for the target consumer segment. Confidential business strategy document used in brand planning, NPD briefs, and agency briefings. Not for external distribution.
reference: Brand Management Framework; FTC Advertising Guidelines

description: Hexadecimal color code for the brand's primary brand color as defined in the brand identity guidelines (e.g., '#FF6B35'). Used for consistent visual identity application across digital marketing, packaging, and trade materials in Salesforce Marketing Cloud and design systems.
reference: Brand Identity Standards; Salesforce Marketing Cloud Content Builder

description: Consumer-facing articulation of the core benefit or commitment the brand makes to its consumers (e.g., 'Real ingredients, real taste'). Used in marketing communications, packaging copy, and Salesforce Marketing Cloud campaign messaging. Must comply with FTC advertising claim substantiation requirements.
reference: FTC 16 CFR Part 255 Advertising Claims; FDA 21 CFR Part 101 Labeling

description: Current status of regulatory approval for the brand's product claims and labeling across applicable jurisdictions. 'approved' = all required regulatory approvals obtained; 'pending' = applications submitted, awaiting decision; 'under_review' = regulatory authority review in progress; 'rejected' = approval denied, remediation required; 'not_required' = no regulatory approval needed for this brand type. Managed in Veeva Vault Quality Management.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101; EFSA EU Food Safety Regulation; USDA FSIS; Veeva Vault QMS; FSMA 2011

description: Sub-category or segment within the broader product category (e.g., 'Salty Snacks', 'Energy Drinks', 'Greek Yogurt', 'Plant-Based'). Used for granular market share analysis via IRI/SPINS syndicated data and for internal portfolio segmentation reporting.
reference: Nielsen/IRI Segment Taxonomy; SPINS Specialty Product Intelligence

description: Primary social media handle or username for the brand's official social media presence (e.g., '@SunCrispSnacks'). Used for social media monitoring, influencer partnership management, and Salesforce Marketing Cloud social engagement tracking.
reference: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Social Studio; FTC Influencer Marketing Guidelines 16 CFR Part 255

description: Sustainability or environmental claim associated with the brand (e.g., 'Carbon Neutral', 'Rainforest Alliance Certified', 'B Corp Certified', 'Recyclable Packaging'). Must be substantiated per FTC Green Guides (16 CFR Part 260) and EPA environmental compliance standards. Supports ESG reporting and consumer engagement.
reference: FTC Green Guides 16 CFR Part 260; EPA Environmental Compliance; ISO 14001

description: Commercial positioning tier of the brand within the portfolio pricing and consumer value ladder. 'super_premium' = ultra-high-end, limited distribution; 'premium' = above-mainstream RSP positioning; 'mainstream' = mass-market EDLP positioning; 'value' = price-entry or economy positioning. Drives RSP strategy, trade promotion investment, and retail planogram placement.
reference: Revenue Management and Pricing Strategy; Nielsen/IRI Price Tier Classification

description: Date on which the brand's trademark registration expires and must be renewed to maintain legal protection. Used for IP lifecycle management and legal compliance tracking. Triggers renewal workflows in legal management systems.
reference: USPTO Trademark Renewal Requirements; EUIPO Trademark Renewal; WIPO Madrid Protocol

description: Official trademark registration number issued by the relevant intellectual property authority (e.g., USPTO, EUIPO, WIPO). Used for IP management, legal protection, and brand licensing agreements. Confidential business identifier managed in legal systems.
reference: USPTO Trademark Act 15 USC 1051; EUIPO EU Trademark Regulation 2017/1001; WIPO Madrid Protocol

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the brand record in the enterprise data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601 Date/Time Standard; FSMA Record-Keeping Requirements 21 CFR Part 1.337

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each brand equity measurement record in the tracker. Primary key for the brand_equity_tracker data product.
Reference to the brand being measured in this equity tracking record. Links to the brand master data entity.

Reference to the consumer segment (e.g., millennials, health-conscious, heavy users) for which brand equity metrics are reported. Enables segment-level brand health analysis.

Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Media planning is built around promotion plans; linking enables plan‑level media budgeting.

Current lifecycle state of the media plan within the approval and execution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|paused|cancelled|closed — promote to reference product]

description: Geographic region code for regional budget planning (e.g., NORAM, EMEA, APAC, LATAM). Enables regional financial target setting and variance analysis.
Boolean flag indicating whether this budget line represents a carry-forward from a previous fiscal period. True = Carry-forward budget, False = New budget allocation.

Boolean flag indicating whether the budget record is locked for editing. True = Locked (no further changes allowed), False = Unlocked (editable). Used to freeze budgets after approval or period close.
Timestamp when this budget record was last updated in the lakehouse. Follows format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Free-text notes or comments providing additional context, assumptions, or justification for the budget allocation. Used for documenting budget rationale and planning assumptions.
Manufacturing plant or facility code to which this budget applies. Used for plant-specific budget allocation in manufacturing and operations.
Planned quantity associated with the budget (e.g., headcount, production volume, units sold). Used for non-monetary budget planning such as workforce or production capacity.
Unit of measure for the budget quantity (e.g., FTE for headcount, MT for metric tons, EA for each). Provides context for the budget_quantity field.
Sales organization code for revenue and sales-related budgets. Represents the sales division or region responsible for revenue targets.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD - Sales Organization Master

description: Primary key or unique identifier of the budget record in the source system. Used for traceability and reconciliation back to the source system.
Identifier of the source system from which this budget record originated (e.g., SAP_CO, ORACLE_EPM, ANAPLAN). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Code identifying the version of the budget (e.g., ORIGINAL, REVISED, LE1, LE2, FORECAST). Enables tracking of budget iterations and revisions throughout the fiscal year.
reference: SAP CO Budget Management - Version Control

description: Descriptive name for the budget version (e.g., Original Budget, Q2 Revised Budget, Latest Estimate). Provides human-readable context for the version code.

Unique surrogate identifier for the company code record in the Food Beverage lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity. Role classification: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a legal entity (incorporated company, subsidiary, joint venture, or holding entity) that the business owns and manages within the Food Beverage group.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master

description: The primary accounting standard under which this company code prepares its statutory financial statements. Determines recognition, measurement, and disclosure rules for revenue, inventory (COGS/FIFO), fixed assets, and trade spend accruals. Drives parallel ledger configuration in SAP.
reference: IFRS Foundation; US GAAP FASB ASC; SAP S/4HANA FI Parallel Ledger Configuration

description: City of the registered office of the legal entity. Used in statutory filings, regulatory submissions, and legal correspondence. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Address; FDA 21 CFR

description: Postal or ZIP code of the registered office of the legal entity. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, regulatory filings, and statutory correspondence.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Address; ISO 3166-1

description: State, province, or region of the registered office. Used for sub-national tax jurisdiction determination, state-level regulatory compliance (e.g., California Prop 65 for food labeling), and regional statutory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (REGIO); US State Tax Authorities; California Proposition 65

description: Street address of the registered office of the legal entity as recorded in the official company registry. Used for statutory correspondence, regulatory filings with FDA/USDA/EFSA, and legal notices. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Address; FDA 21 CFR; EFSA EU Regulation 178/2002

description: SAP business area code representing the operational division or segment within the Food Beverage group (e.g., North America Snacks, EMEA Beverages, APAC Dairy). Used for segment-level financial reporting, cross-company-code balance sheet preparation, and divisional P&L analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Business Area; IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: The SAP chart of accounts key assigned to this company code, defining the general ledger account structure used for financial postings. Governs the GL account hierarchy, COGS account assignments, revenue accounts, and trade spend accrual accounts. Maps to SAP KTOPL field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (KTOPL); IFRS IAS 1; US GAAP ASC 205

description: The four-character alphanumeric SAP FI company code that uniquely identifies the legal entity within the ERP system. This is the externally-known business identifier used across all SAP modules (FI, CO, MM, SD, PP) and in intercompany transactions, statutory reporting, and financial consolidation. Maps directly to the BUKRS field in SAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI — BUKRS; IFRS IAS 27 Consolidated Financial Statements

description: Current lifecycle status of the company code within the Food Beverage group. Controls whether the entity is available for posting, planning, and reporting. Inactive or dormant entities are excluded from operational processes but retained for historical audit and statutory purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master; IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Official company registration number issued by the national company registry (e.g., Companies House in the UK, SEC in the US, Handelsregister in Germany). Used for statutory filings, regulatory submissions, and legal entity verification in trade agreements.
reference: SEC Regulation S-K; Companies Act 2006 (UK); SAP S/4HANA FI Legal Entity Master

description: The consolidation group or reporting unit to which this company code belongs within the Food Beverage group financial consolidation structure. Used in SAP Group Reporting or BPC to aggregate financial results for segment reporting, EBITDA roll-ups, and statutory consolidated accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting; IFRS 10; US GAAP ASC 810

description: Flag indicating whether this company code is included in the Food Beverage group consolidated financial statements. Entities with this flag set to false (e.g., dormant shells, entities below materiality threshold) are excluded from group EBITDA reporting and multi-entity consolidation runs.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; US GAAP ASC 810 Consolidation

description: SAP Controlling (CO) area to which this company code is assigned. The controlling area defines the organizational unit for management accounting, cost center accounting, profit center accounting, COGS variance analysis, and standard costing. A single controlling area may span multiple company codes for cross-entity cost allocation. Maps to SAP KOKRS field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (KOKRS); IFRS IAS 2 Inventories; US GAAP ASC 330

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code representing the country in which the legal entity is incorporated and registered. Determines applicable tax jurisdiction, regulatory reporting requirements (FDA, EFSA, USDA), and statutory accounting standards.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAP S/4HANA FI (LAND1); EFSA EU Food Safety Regulation; FDA 21 CFR

description: SAP credit control area assigned to this company code, governing customer credit limit management, accounts receivable exposure monitoring, and credit risk controls for trade customers. Relevant for revenue management and trade spend credit exposure. Maps to SAP KKBER field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR (KKBER); IFRS IFRS 9 Financial Instruments

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the local (functional) currency of the company code. All financial postings, COGS calculations, standard costing, and statutory financial statements for this entity are denominated in this currency. Maps to SAP WAERS field.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI (WAERS); IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: The date on which the company code was deactivated or blocked for new postings in SAP FI. Null for currently active entities. Used to manage the lifecycle of dormant, liquidated, or divested entities within the Food Beverage group and to control consolidation scope.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master; IFRS 5 Non-current Assets Held for Sale

description: Classification of the legal entity within the Food Beverage corporate group structure. Drives consolidation treatment, intercompany elimination rules, and statutory reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: subsidiary|joint_venture|holding|branch|associate|operating_entity — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; IFRS 11 Joint Arrangements

description: Identifier of the SAP S/4HANA or Oracle Cloud ERP system instance from which this company code record is sourced. Relevant for Food Beverage entities that operate across multiple ERP landscapes (e.g., post-acquisition entities not yet migrated to the central SAP instance). Supports data lineage and source system traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA System Landscape; Oracle Cloud ERP; Food Beverage Enterprise Architecture Standards

description: SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal calendar structure for this company code — including whether the fiscal year is calendar-year aligned or offset (e.g., April–March, October–September), and the number of posting periods. Critical for period-end close, budgeting cycles, and demand planning alignment. Maps to SAP PERIV field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (PERIV); IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Regulatory license or registration number issued by the applicable food safety authority (e.g., FDA Food Facility Registration Number, USDA Establishment Number, EFSA authorization number) for this legal entity. Required for manufacturing, importing, or distributing food and beverage products. Critical for FSMA compliance and GFSI certification tracking.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Food Facility Registration; FSMA; USDA FSIS; EFSA EU Regulation 178/2002; GFSI

description: Flag indicating whether the local currency of this company code is also its functional currency as defined under IFRS IAS 21. When false, a separate functional currency determination is required, impacting foreign currency translation adjustments in the consolidated financial statements.
reference: IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Configuration

business_glossary_term: Global Food Safety Initiative (GFSI) Certification Scheme
description: The GFSI-benchmarked food safety certification scheme held by this legal entity. Indicates the food safety management standard under which the entity operates (e.g., SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000). Used for supplier qualification, customer compliance requirements, and regulatory audit readiness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQF|BRC|FSSC_22000|IFS|GlobalGAP|not_certified — promote to reference product]
reference: GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; SQF Code; BRC Global Standard; FSSC 22000; ISO 22000

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the Food Beverage group reporting currency used in consolidated financial statements, EBITDA reporting, and multi-entity consolidation. Typically USD or EUR for global food and beverage groups. Enables currency translation for intercompany eliminations.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA FI Consolidation Currency

business_glossary_term: Goods and Services Tax (GST) Applicable Indicator
description: Flag indicating whether Goods and Services Tax (GST) or equivalent consumption tax applies to transactions of this company code. Relevant for entities operating in GST jurisdictions (e.g., Australia, Canada, India, Singapore). Drives tax code determination in SAP SD and MM for food and beverage product sales.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration; GST Act (Australia, India, Canada); Codex Alimentarius Commission

description: The date on which the legal entity was formally incorporated and registered with the relevant national company registry. Used for entity age calculations, statutory anniversary filings, and historical financial consolidation scope determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Legal Entity Master; IFRS IAS 27; SEC Regulation S-K

description: Flag indicating whether this company code participates in intercompany transactions within the Food Beverage group. When true, the entity is subject to intercompany elimination rules during group consolidation, transfer pricing policies, and intercompany billing processes. Maps to SAP intercompany configuration.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA FI Intercompany Configuration

description: Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the primary language used for correspondence, statutory documents, and regulatory filings for this company code. Drives language-specific output for invoices, financial statements, and FDA/EFSA regulatory submissions.
reference: ISO 639-1; SAP S/4HANA FI (SPRAS); FDA 21 CFR Labeling Requirements

description: SAP ledger group assigned to this company code, enabling parallel accounting under multiple accounting standards (e.g., IFRS and local GAAP simultaneously). Supports multi-GAAP reporting requirements for Food Beverage entities operating across jurisdictions with different statutory requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI New General Ledger; IFRS IAS 1; US GAAP ASC 205

description: The full registered legal name of the incorporated entity as it appears in the official company registry of the country of incorporation. Used for statutory reporting, regulatory filings, intercompany agreements, and financial consolidation disclosures.
reference: IFRS IAS 27; SEC Regulation S-X; SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master (BUTXT)

description: SAP posting period variant that controls which fiscal periods are open or closed for financial postings in this company code. Governs the financial close process, period-end accruals, and trade spend accrual postings. Maps to SAP XVPER field.

Default SAP profit center assigned to this company code for management accounting purposes. Profit centers align to business units (e.g., Snacks, Beverages, Dairy) and are used for internal P&L reporting, EBITDA analysis by segment, and revenue management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA Profit Center Accounting; IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: Timestamp when the company code master record was first created in the source ERP system (SAP FI or Oracle Cloud ERP). Represents the business creation event, not the lakehouse ingestion time. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and entity lifecycle tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Audit Log; ISO 8601; Food Beverage Data Governance Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the company code master record in the source ERP system. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental lakehouse loads, and audit trail compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Change Documents; ISO 8601; Food Beverage Data Governance Standards

description: Abbreviated or trading name of the legal entity used in internal reporting, dashboards, and operational communications where the full legal name is impractical. Corresponds to the SAP company code short description field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this company code master record was ingested into the lakehouse. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements for the Silver layer. Values: SAP_S4 (SAP S/4HANA FI), ORACLE_CLOUD (Oracle Cloud ERP Financials), LEGACY (pre-migration legacy ERP).
reference: Food Beverage Data Governance Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Architecture

description: Tax jurisdiction code identifying the specific federal, state/province, and local tax authority applicable to this company code. Used for automated tax calculation on sales orders, purchase orders, and intercompany transactions. Drives compliance with FDA, USDA, and local food safety levy requirements. Maps to SAP TXJCD field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (TXJCD); US Internal Revenue Code; OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines

description: Employer Identification Number (EIN) or equivalent national tax identification number assigned to the legal entity by the relevant tax authority. Used for statutory tax filings, payroll tax reporting, and regulatory submissions to FDA and USDA.
reference: US IRS EIN; OECD Common Reporting Standard (CRS); SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Master

description: The OECD-approved transfer pricing method applied to intercompany transactions involving this company code. Governs how intercompany prices are set for goods (e.g., finished snacks, beverages transferred between manufacturing and distribution entities) and services. Critical for tax compliance and EBITDA integrity across the group. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CUP|resale_price|cost_plus|TNMM|profit_split|not_applicable — promote to reference product]
reference: OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines 2022; IFRS IAS 24 Related Party Disclosures; US IRC Section 482

description: Value Added Tax (VAT) registration number issued by the tax authority of the country of incorporation. Required for EU VAT compliance, cross-border trade invoicing, and regulatory reporting to EFSA and national tax authorities. Used in intercompany billing and trade documentation.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; EFSA EU Food Safety Regulation; SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the cost center record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents an organizational unit the business owns and manages for cost allocation purposes.
reference: SAP CO (Controlling) Module — Cost Center Master Data

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: cost_center is defined under a legal entity; replace string code with FK to company_code

Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Cost centers are assigned to specific production facilities; linking to establishment registration enables facility‑level cost tracking for regulatory compliance.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Cost centers are assigned to profit centers for internal P&L reporting — costs incurred by a cost center are attributed to its profit center. The existing profit_center_code (STRING) in cost_center is

Primary activity type code associated with this cost center, representing the unit of output used for direct activity allocation (e.g., machine hours, labor hours, production runs). Used in standard costing and variance analysis. Maps to SAP CO activity type (LSTAR).
reference: SAP CO — Activity Type Master (KL01); IFRS IAS 2

description: Method used to allocate or distribute costs from this cost center to other cost objects (orders, products, other cost centers). Drives COGS calculation accuracy and variance analysis. 'direct_activity' uses activity types; 'assessment' and 'distribution' use cycle-based allocation.
reference: SAP CO — Allocation Cycles (KSV5/KSU5); IFRS IAS 2 (Cost Allocation)

description: Approved annual budget for this cost center in the cost center currency for the current fiscal year. Used as the baseline for budget vs. actual variance reporting and EBITDA forecasting. Sourced from SAP CO budget planning (KPF6).
reference: SAP CO — Cost Center Budget (KPF6); IFRS IAS 1 (Management Commentary)

description: The fiscal year (four-digit integer, e.g., 2025) to which the budget_amount applies. Enables multi-year budget tracking and year-over-year variance analysis.
reference: SAP CO — Cost Center Budget Planning; IFRS IAS 1

description: SAP business area code representing a distinct area of operations or responsibility within the company for internal balance sheet and P&L reporting. Supports segment-level financial reporting across legal entities. Maps to SAP field GSBER.
reference: SAP FI — Business Area Configuration; IFRS IFRS 8

description: SAP user ID of the person who last modified this cost center master record in SAP CO. Supports change audit trail and Sarbanes-Oxley internal control documentation. Maps to SAP field AENAM.
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Changed By; SOX Section 404; ISO 9001

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Relevant Flag
description: Indicates whether costs incurred by this cost center are included in COGS calculation (True) or classified as operating expenses (False). Production and quality cost centers are typically COGS-relevant; administrative and marketing cost centers are not.
reference: SAP CO — COGS Allocation; IFRS IAS 2 (Inventories — Cost Inclusion); FDA 21 CFR Part 110

description: SAP Controlling (CO) area code that governs this cost center. The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost accounting is performed. All cost centers within a controlling area share the same chart of accounts and fiscal year variant. Maps to SAP field KOKRS.
reference: SAP CO Configuration — Controlling Area (OKKP); IFRS IAS 1

description: Functional classification of the cost center indicating its primary business activity. Drives COGS allocation methodology and EBITDA reporting segmentation. Maps to SAP field KOSAR (Cost Center Category). CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Cost Center Category; IFRS IAS 2 (Inventories — production cost allocation)

description: The externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the cost center within the controlling area, as assigned in SAP CO. Used in journal entries, purchase orders, and COGS allocation. Maps to SAP field KOSTL (Cost Center). BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Cost Center Master; IFRS IAS 1 (Presentation of Financial Statements)

description: Long-form description of the cost center's business purpose, scope of operations, and organizational context. Provides additional detail beyond the short name for reporting and governance.
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Cost Center Master

description: Human-readable short name of the cost center (e.g., 'Chicago Snack Plant', 'R&D Lab - Beverages', 'North America Sales Region'). Maps to SAP field KTEXT. IDENTITY_LABEL category.
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Cost Center Master

description: Current lifecycle state of the cost center record. 'locked' prevents new postings; 'pending_deletion' marks records flagged for archival after period close. Maps to SAP deletion flag and lock indicators. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Cost Center Status; IFRS IAS 1

description: Operational classification distinguishing how costs flow: 'direct' cost centers incur costs directly attributable to production (e.g., production lines); 'indirect' and 'overhead' centers accumulate shared costs for allocation; 'service' centers provide internal services charged to other cost centers.
reference: SAP CO — Cost Center Type; IFRS IAS 2 (Cost Absorption Methodology)

description: SAP cost element group associated with this cost center, defining the set of cost elements (G/L accounts) that can be posted to it. Supports cost reporting by cost element category (material, labor, overhead, depreciation).
reference: SAP CO — Cost Element Groups (KAH1); IFRS IAS 1

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this cost center operates (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Used for statutory reporting, transfer pricing compliance, and regulatory cost allocation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IFRS IAS 21; OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines

description: SAP user ID of the person who created this cost center master record in SAP CO. Supports audit trail and change management governance. Maps to SAP field ERNAM.
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Created By; SOX Section 404 (User Access Controls); ISO 9001

description: Timestamp when this cost center master record was first created in the source system (SAP CO). Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage. Maps to SAP field ERDAT/ERZET. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Created On; SOX Section 404 (Audit Trail); ISO 9001

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which costs for this cost center are planned and reported (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determines the currency for budget, actual, and variance postings. Maps to SAP field WAERS.
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Currency; ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 (Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates)

description: Organizational department code to which this cost center belongs (e.g., 'MFG-OPS', 'FIN-CTRL', 'MKT-BRAND'). Supports departmental cost rollup reporting and aligns with HR organizational structure from Workday HCM.
reference: SAP CO — Organizational Assignment; Workday HCM Org Structure

description: SAP fiscal year variant code defining the fiscal calendar applicable to this cost center (e.g., 'K4' for calendar year, 'V3' for April–March). Governs period-end close and budget cycle alignment. Maps to SAP field GJAHR variant.
reference: SAP FI — Fiscal Year Variant (OB29); IFRS IAS 1 (Reporting Period)

description: SAP functional area code classifying the cost center by business function (e.g., manufacturing, sales, administration, R&D) for cost-of-sales accounting and functional income statement reporting. Maps to SAP field FKBER.
reference: SAP CO — Functional Area; IFRS IAS 1 (Income Statement by Function of Expense)

description: Geographic region associated with this cost center (e.g., 'North America', 'Europe', 'Asia Pacific'). Supports regional P&L reporting, trade spend analysis, and sales region cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: north_america|europe|asia_pacific|latin_america|middle_east_africa|global — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP CO — Regional Reporting; IFRS IFRS 8

description: Node identifier within the standard cost center hierarchy (SAP standard hierarchy) to which this cost center belongs. Enables hierarchical cost rollup reporting from individual cost centers to plant, region, and enterprise levels. Maps to SAP field KHINR.
reference: SAP CO — Standard Hierarchy (OKEON); IFRS IAS 1

description: Indicates whether this cost center participates in intercompany cost allocations (True), meaning costs may be charged across legal entities. Triggers intercompany elimination processes during financial consolidation.
reference: SAP CO — Intercompany Allocation; IFRS IAS 27; OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines

description: Indicates whether this cost center is locked (True) to prevent new actual cost postings, typically applied during period-end close or when a cost center is being restructured. Maps to SAP CO lock indicator (SPERRK).
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Lock Indicator; SOX Section 404 (Internal Controls)

description: Indicates whether this cost center is a statistical cost center (True) used only for informational reporting without actual cost postings, or a real cost center (False) that receives actual cost allocations. Maps to SAP CO statistical indicator.
reference: SAP CO — Statistical Cost Centers; IFRS IAS 1

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this cost center master record in the source system. Supports change detection for incremental data loads and audit compliance. Maps to SAP field AEDAT/AEZET.
reference: SAP CO KS01 — Changed On; SOX Section 404; ISO 9001
